Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
We’re on a mission to become the go-to destination for students across Europe to find a trusted home away from home. We make every step of the rental journey simple and seamless, so students can focus on what really matters: studying, exploring, and growing.

With HousingAnywhere, Kamernet, and Studapart under our roof, we’re Europe’s largest student rental platform.

You’ll find us in Rotterdam, Paris, and Cergy, always guided by our four core values: Ownership, We are enablers, We are changemakers, and We are connectors.

Join us in empowering students unlock their potential through a safe, online, rental experience.

Your role & impact

We are looking for a data-driven professional who's excited to transform business questions into actionable insights that drive decisions. In this role, you’ll collaborate with commercial teams, to influence strategic planning and elevate business performance. You will work alongside talented and driven data peers in a dynamic environment to create real impact in HousingAnywhere's growth.

Role

The purpose of this role is to empower commercial teams with insights and recommendations to grow our marketplace and get the most out of our team:


Collaborate closely with the commercial teams to translate business questions into analyses, synthesise insights and develop recommendations to drive business results.
Define, develop and monitor the key metrics to measure operational performance and business outcomes, and identify drivers for changes in these metrics.
Ensure business questions are addressed in the most effective format (ad-hoc deep-dive, recurring analysis, dashboards, self-serving functional tools).
Empower stakeholders to independently leverage data for business insights via dashboards and self-serving tools, in close collaboration with the Analytics Engineering and Business Intelligence teams.
Develop dashboards and reports that support operational business processes.
Support commercial teams' OKR planning and measurement.
Identify and implement opportunities to enhance existing analytical processes and methodologies.


Your team

Our Commercial Analytics and Business Intelligence team is formed by 4 analysts from diverse backgrounds and experience levels. We collaborate closely with data engineers, analytics engineers, and data scientists to extract insights and drive informed decision-making.

Your profile

Required:


4+ years experience as a Data Analyst, or equivalent role with a strong data/analytics component.
Analytical mindset, combined with good business sense and orientation.
A good communicator who can present insights and recommendations clearly and persuasively to different audiences.
Self-starter with strong project and time management skills.
Proficient in SQL and Google Sheets, and experience with at least one dashboarding tool (Tableau, Looker, PowerBI, etc.).


Preferred


Advanced knowledge of at least one statistical programming language (Python, R, etc.) is a plus.
Previous experience in a high-growth, fast-paced tech company is a big plus.
Experience creating interactive dashboards in Tableau.


What’s in it for you


Depending on experience and qualifications, the salary range is €56.200 – €72.000 gross per year (based on 40 hours, including Holiday Allowance).
Unlimited paid holidays, minimum-based not maximum!
Hybrid working policy with 2 days working from the office.
Flexibility to work remotely for up to 2 months per year!
1,000 EUR personal development budget.
Complete coverage for commuting.
30% ruling application assistance.
Personal equipment, including laptop and ergonomic setup.
Gym membership discount with GoVital or OneFit.
Dutch/English classes budget.
Variable pension scheme.
Diverse international community (46+ nationalities).
Pet-friendly office in Rotterdam city centre.
Fun team-building and after-work drinks every Friday.


What can you expect from our hiring process? Read more here.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
HTML:
About the Role:

For a client in Amsterdam, we are looking for a interim Data Analyst. The ideal candidate will use their passion for big data and analytics to provide insights to the business covering a range of topics. They will be responsible for conducting both recurring and ad hoc analysis for business users.


Responsibilities

  • Understand the day-to-day issues that our business faces, which can be better understood with data;
  • Compile and analyse data related to business' issues;
  • Develop clear visualisations to convey complicated data in a straightforward manner


Requirements:

  • 1-3 years of experience;
  • Hands-on experience with SQL;
  • Working experience with Python and/or PySpark;
  • Working experience with Tableau or PowerBI;
  • Stakeholder management and communication skills;
  • Must be based in the Netherlands (sponsorship not offered)


What We Offer:

  • An interim position
  • Flexible Working: Hybrid model, with 2–3 days in the office in Amsterdam (non-negotiable)

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Are you a data analyst with a strong product mindset and a genuine passion for data quality? Do you enjoy translating complex data challenges into actionable business insights — and want to play a central role in improving the data that drives critical processes across a large financial organisation?




We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join a multidisciplinary team within a large banking domain. You will work within the value space 'Data Quality, Governance and Access', where your work directly impacts how customer data is used in key processes such as onboarding, servicing, and regulatory reporting.




Your role

As a Data Analyst, you sit at the intersection of data quality and business impact. You combine strong analytical skills with a product mindset and work closely with stakeholders from across the organisation. This is not a data engineering role, we are looking for someone who can query, analyse, and present data in a way that drives real decisions.




You will:

Monitor and improve data quality by defining and applying data quality rules and controls
Analyse data issues, such as duplicates, missing or incorrect data, and identify root causes across processes and systems
Drive structural improvements by collaborating with upstream and downstream teams
Support consumers of customer data and help them use data effectively
Translate business needs into clear data requirements and user stories
Increase data awareness and promote data quality practices across the organisation
Take ownership of data quality improvement topics and contribute to prioritisation with stakeholders across multiple domains




Your team

You will be part of a cross-functional team responsible for customer data services used bank-wide. You will collaborate with stakeholders across different areas where reliable customer data is critical to daily operations.




What you bring

Strong analytical skills and a data-driven approach to decision making
Proficiency in SQL and experience working with large datasets
Hands-on experience with Power BI for building dashboards and visualising insights
Familiarity with Python (pandas) and/or Excel for deeper data analysis
The ability to communicate effectively with both technical and business stakeholders
A talent for translating data into clear, actionable insights that drive impact
Ownership mentality — you proactively identify and drive improvements within your domain
Comfort working in multidisciplinary, agile teams
Fluency in both Dutch and English (verbal and written)

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst | Amsterdam




Our client is a fast-growing Dutch SaaS company that lets consumers find and buy insurance through a smart app. With roughly 50 people and ambitions to expand across Europe, they're building out the data team that powers every business decision.




What will you be doing?




Build and own the data model that sits underneath every dashboard, report, and analysis in the company.
Set up and maintain data marts so the business always has clean, ready-to-use data at their fingertips.
Own the numbers - from financial close reports to operational updates - and make sure the right people have the right information at the right time.
Dig into the data for Finance and Insurance: spot patterns before anyone asks, agree on what the key metrics mean, and answer the questions that move decisions forward.




Tech stack

SQL · Python · Databricks · QuickSight · AWS




What do you bring?




3–6 years of experience as a data analyst or in a similar role
Strong SQL & Python skills
Python for data transformation and ad-hoc analysis
Hands-on experience with Databricks
Experience building dashboards (QuickSight, Power BI, Tableau or similar)
Comfortable working independently and owning your output




What's in it for you?

€80,000 - €85,000 gross per year, based on experience
Real ownership - small team, visible impact
Working in an environment where things actually get built




What to expect

The role is office-based in Amsterdam (3 days on-site). They operate in a high-performance environment where you'll be expected to work autonomously, get into the detail when needed, and keep up with a team that moves quickly and changes direction when necessary.




Interested and would you like to hear more? Please send me a direct message on either LinkedIn or email me with your most recent CV to: jan@doghouse-recruitment.nl

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Job Description

Data Analyst Intern

Start date: August/September 2026

Duration: 6 months

Allowance: 625 euro per month (40hrs, 5 days) & full reimbursement of travel expenses  

Platform/Project: R&D Digital Transformation Team

Location: Amsterdam/Nijmegen (Primary location: Amsterdam)

Teams: R&D Digital Transformation

R&D Digital Transformation

You will be part of an international small team with the ambition to drive digital technology forward for Kraft Heinz R&D International Zone. Expect to be part of the team from day one. Strong learning experience on data visualization with many practical deliverables which will be used directly by the organization. 

Assignments And Key Activities


Support dashboard creation to support day to day work of R&D functions 
Support digital transformation across the R&D team (e.g. communication about changes- identification of digital tools required by R&D team) 
Investigate new tools and trends in Digital world 
Participate & contribute into meetings related to Digital strategy 
Collaboration with Digital Ambassadors 


Basic Requirements For All

You should have strong communication skills and fluency in English, both written and verbal (Dutch is not a requirement but other languages are a plus) 


EU - Students: Need to be enrolled at a Dutch university for at least 50% of the duration of the internship.
Non - EU Students: Need to be enrolled at a Dutch University for the whole duration of the internship.


NOTE: EU working rights are required (Either you have EU Citizenship, you are studying in the Netherlands or have relevant student work visa) 

Specific Team Requirements


Master’s or Bachelor’s student in Business Analytics, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field.
Proficiency in SQL
Data visualization skills (Power BI is a big plus)
Being comfortable with working with big data
Having a structured personality with ability to learn fast
Previous internship with data visualization or experience with data analytics is a plus


Recruitment Process


CV review – We screen applications for key eligibility criteria (e.g., EU working rights).
Online Pymetrics assessment
Screening interview – An initial interview conducted via Microsoft Teams.
Hiring Manager interview
Offer – Successful candidates will receive an offer to join the program.


Please note: The recruitment process can take 4–6 weeks in total, from application to offer.

What We Offer You

We take interns seriously. You can expect us to give you 1-on-1 mentorship from one of our experienced colleagues. We will work together on your personal learning goals and are keen to hear all the good ideas you have! We will give you the space and support to make mistakes and grow. Become a part of a close intern community as well as a young and ambitious work environment (including online).

As a Kraft Heinz intern you’ll be the owner of your own projects and especially of your own career. At Kraft Heinz, we provide the playing field. You change the game. Are you up for the challenge?   

About Us 

The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. We’re a globally trusted producer of high-quality, great-tasting, and nutritious foods for over 150 years. Our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands. 

We spark joy around mealtime with our iconic brands, including Heinz, Kraft, Bull's-Eye, HP, Lea and Perrins, Quero, ABC, Master, Banquete, Orlando, Benedicta, Honig, Pudliszki, Wattie’s among others. 

No matter the brand, we’re united under one vision: To sustainably grow by delighting more consumers globally. Bringing this vision to life is our team of 39,000+ food lovers, creative thinkers, and high performers worldwide. Together, we help provide meals to those in need through our global partnership with Rise Against Hunger. We also stand committed to responsible, sustainable practices that extend to every facet of our business, our consumers, and our communities. Every day, we’re transforming the food industry with bold thinking and unprecedented results. If you share our passion – and are ready to create the future, build a legacy, and lead as a global citizen – there’s only one thing to do: join our table and let’s make life delicious! 

Our Culture of Ownership, Meritocracy & Collaboration 

We're not afraid to think differently. Embrace new ideas. Dream big. We empower our people at every level – from entry-level intern to senior leader – to own their work. We share a responsibility to think like Owners – to be mindful of the collective and sustained success of Kraft Heinz – which we apply to every situation, every day. As part of Kraft Heinz, you're supported to grow and achieve. You’re expected to bring your authentic self to work every day, to lead with humility, and drive outstanding performance at every level – and you’ll be rewarded. You’re given opportunities to leave a mark and build a legacy. But you won’t do it alone.  You’re supported by passionate teammates along the way, and our collective, collaborative spirit fuels our incredible progress. 

Location(s)

Amsterdam

Kraft Heinz is an Equal Opportunity Employer – Underrepresented Ethnic Minority Groups/Women/Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity and other protected classes.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Amsterdam, Netherlands | €55,000 to €65,000




This role offers the chance to work at the heart of a large commercial organisation where data directly informs strategy, performance, and customer experience. You will partner closely with stakeholders across commercial functions, turning complex data into clear insights that drive measurable impact.




The Company

They are a well established organisation operating at scale within a commercial, customer focused environment. Data and analytics are central to how they optimise marketing, sales, service, and digital performance. The business continues to invest in modern data platforms and values collaboration between analytics, technology, and the wider commercial teams.




The Role

Analyse and interpret data from multiple commercial sources to support decision making
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports used by senior stakeholders
Translate business questions into structured analytical approaches
Deliver ad hoc analysis and deep dives to uncover trends and opportunities
Contribute to improving data quality, consistency, and documentation




Your Skills & Experience

Strong commercial experience in a Data Analyst or analytics focused role
Confident using SQL to query and manipulate complex datasets
Experience with BI and data visualisation tools
Ability to clearly communicate insights to non technical audiences
Comfortable working across teams in a fast paced, data driven environment




What They Offer

Salary between €55,000 and €65,000 depending on experience
Flexible working arrangements
Exposure to high impact, cross functional projects
Clear development opportunities within analytics and data




How to Apply

Apply now to take the next step in your Data Analyst career within a commercially focused data environment.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




As a preferred supplier to one of our biggest Financial Clients, I am seeking for a Data Analyst for a position in Amsterdam, Netherlands. As a Data Analyst, you play a key role in improving and safeguarding the quality of customer data. You combine analytical skills with a product mindset and work closely with stakeholders across the bank.




Responsibilities:

Monitor and improve data quality by defining and applying data quality rules and controls
Analyze data issues (e.g. duplicates, missing or incorrect data) and identify root causes across processes and systems
Drive structural improvements by collaborating with upstream and downstream teams
Support consumers of customer data and help them use data effectively
Translate business needs into clear data requirements and user stories
Increase data awareness and promote data quality practices within the bank




Required knowledge and experience

Have strong analytical skills and take decisions based on data and insights
Are proficient in SQL and experienced in working with large datasets
Have hands-on experience with Power BI for building dashboards and visualizing insights
Are familiar with analytical tools such as Python (pandas) and/or Excel for deeper data analysis
Communicate effectively with both technical and business stakeholders
Translate data into clear, actionable insights that drive impact
Take ownership within your domain and proactively drive improvements
Thrive in multidisciplinary, agile teams
Are fluent in Dutch and English (both verbal and written)




Contract: 12 months+

Rates: Excellent

Location: Amsterdam

Interview: 2 stages, 1 technical + 1 assessment

  • Translate data into clear, actionable insights that drive impact
  • Take ownership within your domain and proactively drive improvements
  • Thrive in multidisciplinary, agile teams
  • Are fluent in Dutch and English (both verbal and written)


Contract: 12 months+

Rates: Excellent

Location: Amsterdam

Interview: 2 stages, 1 technical + 1 assessment

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
For ROSEN Europe BV in Oldenzaal, we are looking for two analytical and ambitious Data Analysts with 0–3 years of experience.




Due to the continued growth of our organization and increasing demand for data-driven insights, we currently have two vacancies available within our Data Analytics team.

This is a broad data-driven role within an organization that is rapidly evolving in the areas of data and digitization. You will have the opportunity to help build this further and make a direct impact on how data is utilized within the organization.




Description




In this role, the focus is on analyzing, structuring, and visualizing data, as well as developing dashboards and reports. You will work with data from various systems and translate it into insights used for better decision-making and tangible improvements within the organization. At the same time, you will be involved in improving processes, systems, and ways of working, ensuring that data is effectively applied in practice.

Depending on your experience and profile, the exact role will be tailored within the organization. This could be more corporate-focused (central data, reporting, and strategic analyses) or positioned in one of our Regions responsible for Europe, Central Asia, and Africa.




ROSEN is an international and innovative organization focused on high-end technology and data-driven solutions for industrial applications. Within ROSEN Europe, a culture of quality, innovation, and continuous development is key. The organization is experiencing strong growth and offers ample opportunities for initiative, development, and long-term career progression.




Your tasks




• Conduct data analyses and develop dashboards and reports (Power BI and similar tools)

• Combine, structure, and analyze data from different systems

• Identify trends, patterns, and deviations and translate these into actionable insights and improvements for the organization

• Actively contribute to improving data quality, reporting, and data-driven decision-making

• Support teams and management with clear analyses and practical insights

• Identify and implement improvements in processes and ways of working, including through automation

• Write and optimize policies and documentation, supported by AI tools such as Copilot

• Contribute to various data and business intelligence projects within the organization




What you bring




• Completed Master’s degree in, for example, Mathematics, Business IT, Data Science, or a comparable field

• Strong analytical skills and a high level of abstract thinking

• Experience or strong interest in data analytics and AI tools

• Good knowledge of Excel and Power BI (SQL or similar tools is a plus)

• Affinity with AI tools such as Copilot and modern data practices

• Excellent communication skills and a team-oriented mindset

• Eager to learn, independent, and proactive

• Able to easily switch between analysis, investigation, and practical process improvements

• Living in the Twente region (or willing to commute within 45 minutes) and fluently speaking Dutch




Please note that, due to the composition and balance of the current team, we are specifically looking for candidates who are fluent in Dutch and already have permanent eligibility to work within the European Union. Please note that an orientation year (zoekjaar) permit does not meet this requirement.




If you do not meet these requirements, we kindly ask you not to apply, as your application cannot be considered for this position.




Offer




• A challenging entry-level position with significant responsibility and impact

• Working within an international, innovative, and fast-growing organization

• Plenty of opportunities for personal development and career growth

• Collaboration with experienced professionals in data, engineering, and business

• A competitive salary and attractive secondary benefits

• A professional yet informal working environment with short communication lines

• A role with long-term prospects and international career opportunities




Company profile




ROSEN is an international and innovative organization focused on high-end technology and data-driven solutions for industrial applications. Within ROSEN Europe, a culture of quality, innovation, and continuous development is key. The organization is experiencing strong growth and offers ample opportunities for initiative, development, and long-term career progression.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Rotterdam Hybrid minimum three days in the office

Salary €65,000 to €80,000




This is an opportunity to step into a highly visible Data Analyst role where your work directly shapes business priorities and decision making. Joining during a period of change, you will have real ownership of projects, strong exposure to stakeholders, and the chance to influence how data is used across the organisation.




The Company

They are a growing organisation operating in a dynamic market, with data playing an increasingly central role in strategic decisions. The business values clear communication, ownership, and practical insights over theoretical analysis. With a lean data setup, they offer a collaborative environment where your impact will be felt quickly.




The Role

Deliver actionable insights through business intelligence and data analysis
Own and manage analytical projects from requirement gathering through to delivery
Work closely with stakeholders to understand needs, set priorities, and challenge assumptions
Build and maintain SQL based data models feeding Power BI dashboards
Balance larger strategic initiatives with shorter, ad hoc analytical requests




Your Skills and Experience

Strong commercial experience using SQL for analysis and data modelling
Proven capability building insights and dashboards in Power BI
Confident stakeholder management and the ability to translate data into clear recommendations
Comfortable working independently and taking ownership of priorities
Experience operating in a lean or fast moving environment
Exposure to Python or advanced analytics is beneficial but not essential




What They Offer

Salary between €65,000 and €80,000 depending on experience
Hybrid working with flexibility and a strong on site culture in Rotterdam
A role with genuine ownership and visibility across the business
Career development as the data function continues to evolve




How to Apply

Apply now to explore this Data Analyst opportunity and discuss how your skills could make an impact.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




We bouwen met Just Good People een nextgen data team met leuke mensen met sterke data, AI, human & biz skills. Geen PPT-consultancy, maar datakansen kraken en echt waarde realiseren bij toffe klanten. En veel opleiding.




Zie jij in bergen info – naast getallen – vooral kansen? Wil je die liever omzetten in actie dan in rapporten die in een la verdwijnen?




Wil je maximaal leren door in korte tijd bij meerdere bedrijven in de keuken te kijken? Terwijl wij je helpen ontwikkelen op het snijvlak van data, business en people?




Dan hebben we de uitdaging voor jou.




🟡 Just Good People

Bij JGP hebben we twee simpele regels: We nemen goede mensen aan. En we zorgen goed voor deze mensen. Dat betekent dat we samen optrekken om jou maximaal in je kracht te krijgen op een manier die bij jou past.




🌱 Unieke data filosofie

“As machines get better at being machines, humans have to get better at being more human.”

Deze nieuwe tijd vraagt om een nieuw soort data professional. Bij Just Good People Data helpen we bedrijven versnellen met top notch data professionals met goed ontwikkelde data, business én human skills. En trainen je naar nog hoger niveau. Met aandacht voor:




Volgende levels in Data | AI
“Waarde denken” voor klanten/ gebruikers
Stakeholder management
Communiceren & inspireren




⚡ Wat je gaat doen

Je werkt aan projecten bij mooie bedrijven zoals Bol.com, Defensie, Action, Rabo, NS, AMC, ASML, Essent.




Denk aan:




Het maken van veel impact met data
Stakeholders meenemen in de kracht van data – op een begrijpelijke manier
Verbinden van mensen, processen en tech
Vertalen van data naar heldere inzichten, dashboards en rapportages die helpen bij besluitvorming
Signaleren van trends, kansen en verbeterpunten en stakeholders adviseren op basis van data
Meedenken over slimme oplossingen voor complexe vraagstukken




👩 Wie je bent




Je bent technisch sterk maar ook nieuwsgierig naar de mens achter de vraag
Je vindt het leuk om complexe vraagstukken simpel te maken
Je krijgt energie van verandering en nieuwe omgevingen
Je wilt blijven groeien in data, business én human skills
Je hebt 4+ jaar ervaring als Data Analist
Je hebt een WO-opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in Data Science of Econometrie
Je bent ondernemend en denkt in zowel risico’s als kansen
Je schakelt gemakkelijk tussen strategie, business en data
Je durft vragen te stellen en bestaande werkwijzen uit te dagen
Je bent een echte teamspeler en communicatief sterk
Je spreekt en schrijft vloeiend Nederlands en Engels




🚀 Dit ga je krijgen

Vast dienstverband met goed salaris, bonusregeling en aantrekkelijke voorwaarden
Veel persoonlijke aandacht voor waardegroei, inzetbaarheid en “in je kracht komen“ als data professional. Maar ook data inhoudelijke mentoring.
Opleiding en kruisbestuiving met andere professionals in state of the art AI skills
De kans om in diverse keukens te kijken en veel te leren
Leaseauto of NS-Businesscard (ook privégebruik)
Gezellige borrels, kennissessies en team-events
Collega’s die naast slimme analyses ook goed zijn in slechte grappen




Wij zoeken goede data-mensen, die goede dingen doen. Van die types die je er gewoon heel graag bij wil hebben – net zo goed met mensen als met code!

Title: Operations Data Analyst
Description:
Ready to play a key role in transforming how Nike uses data to drive business decisions? As a Data & Tooling Specialist, you will enable “insights to action” by building scalable reporting, automations, and dashboards that support business reviews and operational excellence.We’re looking for someone analytical, structured, and hands-on, who enjoys translating complex business needs into practical data solutions and tooling that drives clarity and impact.




This is a temporary assignment until 31 December 2026.




What are you going to do?

You will support EBP Operations by improving POS and Bookings reporting, while building scalable tools that enhance business reviews, decision-making, and follow‑through.

Streamline weekly RSV trend reporting, reducing manual effort and improving clarity for executive audiences
Automate POS (Sports x Marketplace) and Bookings reporting, eliminating manual “cut & paste” processes
Translate business requirements into scalable data models, workflows, and dashboards
Build and optimize dashboards (e.g., Answers, Tableau, Power BI) to drive actionable insights
Develop Airtable solutions (meeting tracking, actions, decisions, timelines) as a single source of truth
Set up automations (reminders, status tracking, notifications) to improve process discipline and follow‑up
Ensure data quality, consistency, and repeatable refresh logic across reporting and tooling




What do we offer?

Competitive salary (to be discussed with the recruiter)
Flexible hybrid work: 3 days in the office, 2 days from home
Unlimited vacation days to recharge whenever you need
8% holiday allowance
STIPP pension plan to secure your future
€0.23/km commuting allowance (up to €23 per office day)
€5 daily work-from-home allowance
25% employee store discount – gear up with Nike!
Access to Adecco Academy online learning for continuous growth
Free use of on-campus gym facilities to stay active
Bike plan to make your commute greener and healthier




Where will you work?

Nike, Inc., a global leader in athletic footwear, apparel, and innovation, is headquartered in Beaverton, Oregon, and serves as a beacon of inspiration for athletes worldwide. Its European Headquarters (EHQ) is located on a vibrant campus that reflects the brand's dynamic and innovative spirit. Surrounded by lush parks, cycling paths, and running tracks, the campus embodies Nike’s commitment to sustainability, health, and community. A melting pot of global talent, it fosters creativity and collaboration, driving forward-thinking ideas that shape the future of sports, culture, and lifestyle.




What do we ask?

Strong analytical mindset with experience translating business needs into data solutions
Solid SQL skills and experience working with data platforms such as Databricks or Snowflake
Experience with dashboarding tools (Answers, Tableau, Power BI) to deliver clear insights
Hands-on experience with Airtable (or similar tools), including workflows and automations
Ability to build scalable, low‑maintenance solutions rather than one-off analyses
Strong stakeholder management skills and ability to run lightweight requirement sessions
Structured, independent way of working with a focus on quality, usability, and impact




Are you interested?

Apply now via the button. If you need more information about the team, the company, the role; feel free to send an email to nike@adecco.nl. We will be more than happy to support!

Title: Data Analyst - Analytics Platform
Description:
Good to know: you can easily save this vacancy using the print button at the top of the page. After the closing date, this vacancy will be removed from our website.


Move beyond dashboards and help build Eneco’s next-generation, scalable (BI-as-code) data platform
Turn OKRs and KPIs into trusted, standardized metrics that power decisions across the entire organization
Empower teams with the tools, governance, and knowledge to make smarter, faster decisions.


Why choose Eneco?

At Eneco, we're on a mission to be climate neutral by 2035, not just for ourselves, but for all our customers. Digital innovation plays a key role in achieving this, and our Analytics Platform team is at the heart of that effort. We aim to empower the organization to become truly data- and metric-driven, helping teams measure their impact through actionable KPIs, integrated into our OKR framework.

To support this, we're looking for a Data Analyst who’s passionate about enabling data-informed decisions across the business. While Power BI is our current visualization platform, we're focused on future-proofing our data landscape and are exploring more scalable and code-first analytics solutions. This is an opportunity to help shape that evolution.

What You’ll Do

As a Data Analyst in the Analytics Platform Team, you will be a key enabler of data-driven decision-making across Eneco and Oxxio. Your role goes beyond dashboards, you will help us drive consistency and clarity in how we measure business outcomes and create reusable, scalable analytics capabilities.

While you will support our existing Power BI infrastructure, your focus will be on building a future-ready analytics environment. You’ll work closely with data professionals, product teams, and business stakeholders to align metrics to our OKRs, improve accessibility to trusted insights, and make sure analytics tools and processes evolve alongside our ambitions.

Is this about you?

You are an forward-looking Data Analyst who sees tools like Power BI as enablers — not end goals. Someone who wants to help Eneco evolve toward a mature, scalable, and metrics-focused analytics practice. A team player who enjoys empowering others and contributing to an enterprise-wide transformation.

Must Have:


Strong analytical skills and a structured approach to building KPI- and OKR-driven reporting.
Experience using Power BI in a professional environment.
Understanding of data modeling and performance optimization for analytics products.
Experience in supporting and enabling others with reporting and data literacy.
Solid communication skills, able to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Worked with dbt or similar data modeling tools.


Nice to Have:


Experience with Microsoft Fabric and/or Power BI Premium, and familiarity with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake or Azure.
Understanding of CI/CD and automation for BI tools (e.g., PowerShell, REST APIs).
Experience with other BI tools (Tableau, Looker) or BI-as-code environments.
Experience in Agile/Scrum/Kanban settings.
Dutch language skills.


You’ll be responsible for


Contributing to the future direction of our analytics platform, including the exploration of BI-as-code or alternative visualization tools.
Enabling the organization to become more metric- and KPI-driven by designing and maintaining centralized data models, semantic layers, and reporting standards that ensure consistent, governed metric definitions across teams and tooling.
Owning the governance, administration, and continuous improvement of our BI platform, including capacity, workspace permissions, gateways, and performance, while serving as the primary point of contact for platform-related questions across the organization.
Collaborating with Data Engineering, IT, and other teams to ensure BI and analytics solutions are aligned with the broader data infrastructure and built to be scalable and reusable.
Engaging stakeholders across product, brand, IT, and external partners to gather requirements, advise on tooling decisions, and drive adoption of analytics capabilities.
Enabling teams across the organization to work more effectively with data by driving the adoption of analytics capabilities and best practices through training, documentation, and workshops.


This is where you’ll work

You’ll be part of the Analytics Platform team. Working closely with data engineers, and data analysts who build and maintain foundational capabilities for the entire Eneco organization. The team ensures consistency in tooling and best practices while fostering a strong learning culture.

You’ll also be part of the broader Eneco Data Analytics community, where we support each other’s growth and collaborate across value streams to create business impact.

What We Have To Offer


Gross annual salary between €82.000 and €116.000 Including FlexBudget, 8% holiday allowance, and depending on your role a bonus or collective profit sharing.
FlexBudget Including FlexBudget, 8% holiday allowance, and depending on your role a bonus or collective profit sharing. Have it paid out, use it to buy extra holiday days or save it up for something nice, it's up to you.
Personal and professional growth Eneco is fully committed to help you in your personal and professional development.
Hybrid working: home, office or abroad Work 40% at the office, 40% from home, and 20% flexibly. With manager approval, you may work abroad (within approved countries) up to 3 weeks/year, max 2 consecutively.


Want more information about our terms of employment?

Here's what you get from us

Work Where Everyone Matters

When you choose a career at Eneco, you choose ambition, growth, and opportunity in an environment where everyone matters. You’re given the space to develop yourself and to do your work in a way that suits you. We believe that different perspectives, nationalities, and backgrounds make us stronger, which is why we foster an open, safe and inclusive culture. Naturally, we also prioritize a healthy work-life balance, flexible working hours, and the option to work from home when your role allows it. If you have a physical or sensory disability, we will work with you to find the right adjustments so you can perform your job well.

This is how you build your own future and a sustainable future at the same time. Together with 4,000 colleagues, each with their own talents and ideas, you work on our shared mission: speeding up the energy transition. We help customers become more sustainable faster, create innovative solutions, and seize new opportunities. Will you join us?

The phases of our application procedure

Want to know more about this job function?

Please reach out to our recruiter: Marcha via marcha.crompvoets@eneco.com.

With this job vacancy, we aim to recruit a new colleague for Eneco. Therefore, this is not a solicitation for acquisition.

HTML:
Good to know: you can easily save this vacancy using the print button at the top of the page. After the closing date, this vacancy will be removed from our website.

  • Move beyond dashboards and help build Eneco’s next-generation, scalable (BI-as-code) data platform
  • Turn OKRs and KPIs into trusted, standardized metrics that power decisions across the entire organization
  • Empower teams with the tools, governance, and knowledge to make smarter, faster decisions.

Why choose Eneco?

At Eneco, we're on a mission to be climate neutral by 2035, not just for ourselves, but for all our customers. Digital innovation plays a key role in achieving this, and our Analytics Platform team is at the heart of that effort. We aim to empower the organization to become truly data- and metric-driven, helping teams measure their impact through actionable KPIs, integrated into our OKR framework.

To support this, we're looking for a Data Analyst who’s passionate about enabling data-informed decisions across the business. While Power BI is our current visualization platform, we're focused on future-proofing our data landscape and are exploring more scalable and code-first analytics solutions. This is an opportunity to help shape that evolution.

What You’ll Do

As a Data Analyst in the Analytics Platform Team, you will be a key enabler of data-driven decision-making across Eneco and Oxxio. Your role goes beyond dashboards, you will help us drive consistency and clarity in how we measure business outcomes and create reusable, scalable analytics capabilities.

While you will support our existing Power BI infrastructure, your focus will be on building a future-ready analytics environment. You’ll work closely with data professionals, product teams, and business stakeholders to align metrics to our OKRs, improve accessibility to trusted insights, and make sure analytics tools and processes evolve alongside our ambitions.

Is this about you?

You are an forward-looking Data Analyst who sees tools like Power BI as enablers — not end goals. Someone who wants to help Eneco evolve toward a mature, scalable, and metrics-focused analytics practice. A team player who enjoys empowering others and contributing to an enterprise-wide transformation.

Must Have:

  • Strong analytical skills and a structured approach to building KPI- and OKR-driven reporting.
  • Experience using Power BI in a professional environment.
  • Understanding of data modeling and performance optimization for analytics products.
  • Experience in supporting and enabling others with reporting and data literacy.
  • Solid communication skills, able to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Worked with dbt or similar data modeling tools.

Nice to Have:

  • Experience with Microsoft Fabric and/or Power BI Premium, and familiarity with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake or Azure.
  • Understanding of CI/CD and automation for BI tools (e.g., PowerShell, REST APIs).
  • Experience with other BI tools (Tableau, Looker) or BI-as-code environments.
  • Experience in Agile/Scrum/Kanban settings.
  • Dutch language skills.

You’ll be responsible for

  • Contributing to the future direction of our analytics platform, including the exploration of BI-as-code or alternative visualization tools.
  • Enabling the organization to become more metric- and KPI-driven by designing and maintaining centralized data models, semantic layers, and reporting standards that ensure consistent, governed metric definitions across teams and tooling.
  • Owning the governance, administration, and continuous improvement of our BI platform, including capacity, workspace permissions, gateways, and performance, while serving as the primary point of contact for platform-related questions across the organization.
  • Collaborating with Data Engineering, IT, and other teams to ensure BI and analytics solutions are aligned with the broader data infrastructure and built to be scalable and reusable.
  • Engaging stakeholders across product, brand, IT, and external partners to gather requirements, advise on tooling decisions, and drive adoption of analytics capabilities.
  • Enabling teams across the organization to work more effectively with data by driving the adoption of analytics capabilities and best practices through training, documentation, and workshops.

This is where you’ll work

You’ll be part of the Analytics Platform team. Working closely with data engineers, and data analysts who build and maintain foundational capabilities for the entire Eneco organization. The team ensures consistency in tooling and best practices while fostering a strong learning culture.

You’ll also be part of the broader Eneco Data Analytics community, where we support each other’s growth and collaborate across value streams to create business impact.

What We Have To Offer

  • Gross annual salary between €82.000 and €116.000 Including FlexBudget, 8% holiday allowance, and depending on your role a bonus or collective profit sharing.
  • FlexBudget Including FlexBudget, 8% holiday allowance, and depending on your role a bonus or collective profit sharing. Have it paid out, use it to buy extra holiday days or save it up for something nice, it's up to you.
  • Personal and professional growth Eneco is fully committed to help you in your personal and professional development.
  • Hybrid working: home, office or abroad Work 40% at the office, 40% from home, and 20% flexibly. With manager approval, you may work abroad (within approved countries) up to 3 weeks/year, max 2 consecutively.

Want more information about our terms of employment?

Here's what you get from us

Work Where Everyone Matters

When you choose a career at Eneco, you choose ambition, growth, and opportunity in an environment where everyone matters. You’re given the space to develop yourself and to do your work in a way that suits you. We believe that different perspectives, nationalities, and backgrounds make us stronger, which is why we foster an open, safe and inclusive culture. Naturally, we also prioritize a healthy work-life balance, flexible working hours, and the option to work from home when your role allows it. If you have a physical or sensory disability, we will work with you to find the right adjustments so you can perform your job well.

This is how you build your own future and a sustainable future at the same time. Together with 4,000 colleagues, each with their own talents and ideas, you work on our shared mission: speeding up the energy transition. We help customers become more sustainable faster, create innovative solutions, and seize new opportunities. Will you join us?

The phases of our application procedure

Want to know more about this job function?

Please reach out to our recruiter: Marcha via marcha.crompvoets@eneco.com.

With this job vacancy, we aim to recruit a new colleague for Eneco. Therefore, this is not a solicitation for acquisition.

Title: Junior Data Analyst Retail
Description:
Note: This job posting is only open to native level or near-native level Dutch speakers. If you are not proficient in Dutch, please have a look at our other job postings instead!)

Your role in our team

As a Junior Data Analyst Retail, you support client teams with analysis, dashboards, KPI insights and data quality checks. You work with data from retail processes such as sales, customer behavior, inventory, promotions, e-commerce or supply chain and help make insights understandable for business stakeholders. At CGI, you grow by working in projects with experienced data consultants, project managers and technical specialists.

How You Strengthen The Team


Analyze datasets and translate findings into clear insights and visualizations.
Build and maintain dashboards and reports in Power BI, Tableau or comparable tooling.
Perform basic data quality checks and document definitions, assumptions and issues.
Support requirements, user stories, KPI definitions and stakeholder questions.
Work with senior consultants, data engineers, business analysts and client teams.
Contribute to presentations, workshops and analyses for retail and consumer-services clients.


What You Bring


1-2 years of experience, internship or traineeship in data analysis, BI, business analysis or analytics.
Solid foundation in SQL, Excel and at least one BI tool such as Power BI, Tableau or Looker.
Affinity with retail, e-commerce, supply chain, customer analytics or commercial analysis.
Able to explain data outcomes clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Accurate, eager to learn, communicative and comfortable with client contact.
Professional Dutch and English, or demonstrably quick to develop to that level.


We would also value


Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300) or demonstrable Power BI experience.
Microsoft Azure Data Fundamentals (DP-900), Azure AI Fundamentals (AI-900) or comparable fundamentals.
Basic Python or R for data cleaning, analysis or automation.
Experience with KPIs such as revenue, margin, conversion, inventory, customer segments or promotion effectiveness.
Experience with Jira, Azure DevOps, Confluence or working in an agile team.


Where will you work?

You will join CGI’s retail team and work on assignments where data, business questions and technology come together. You will have room to learn, while contributing from the start to analyses, dashboards and insights that help clients make better decisions.

What We Offer


A learning environment where you can grow quickly towards data consulting in retail.
Guidance from experienced consultants and access to CGI’s broader knowledge network.
Varied assignments where data directly influences commercial and operational decisions.
Room to obtain certifications and further develop your technical and consulting skills.
A team culture centered on collaboration, knowledge sharing and ownership.


Why CGI?

CGI is one of the world's largest IT consultancy firms. Our people keep the Netherlands running. Government agencies, banks, (aero-) space, infrastructure organizations and energy and manufacturing companies are clients of CGI. We work on interesting projects that touch the daily lives of millions of people. We are present regionally, augmented with global domain knowledge, our ecosystem, and colleagues spread all over the world. We are proud of the impactful projects that we, together with our colleagues, make a success.

At CGI, we place great value on diversity and inclusion, as this not only promotes collaboration but often leads to better results. We look forward to your contribution to our team!


Work in small self-managing teams consisting of experts in your field
Take control of your own career with the space to continue developing yourself
You work with the latest technologies
An excellent salary and attractive secondary employment conditions
Immediate permanent contract
Part-time possibilities
And the opportunity to become a CGI shareholder


Join our team of experts and apply now!

We will contact you as soon as possible. Questions about the position? Ask them to Floris Diemel, Director Consulting Services via floris.diemel@cgi.com

For more information about the application procedure, please contact recruiter Jana Kreckova via jana.kreckova@cgi.com

We werken niet samen met externe recruitment bureau’s. Acquisitie wordt daarom niet op prijs gesteld.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.

Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

Title: Pricing Data Analyst
Description:
At Makro we believe in: “SAMEN MAKEN WE MAKRO.”

That's not just a slogan. It's how we work together every day with colleagues and for our customers.



Makro is currently looking for a fulltime PRICING DATA ANALYST located at our HQ Amsterdam.

Makro focuses on low prices, top-quality products, the right assortment, and cost-effective bulk offers. As a Pricing Data Analyst, you play a crucial role in making this vision a reality.



Are you based in region Amsterdam, Netherlands? passionate about data-driven decision-making? Do you have a sharp analytical mind and thrive on creating real commercial impact? If so, we’d love to hear from you!





Job Description



What Can You Expect?

As part of the Pricing Team, your mission will be to continuously improve Makro’s market position across multiple channels (Cash & Carry and Delivery Service).

This involves:



Analyzing market prices, competition and customer needs
In close cooperation with Category Management team identifying best fitting price strategies and price points for our assortment
Preparing analytical reports on article performance, sales and margin developments, to provide valuable insights to the organization and sharpen pricing decisions
Helping to develop and define pricing strategies
Supporting implementation of new pricing tools and processes


Qualifications



Who Are We Looking For?

We’re seeking a dynamic team player. Your commercial insight and analytical skills empower you to make informed decisions that drive tangible results.



Hold a relevant bachelor's degree (HBO level), have 3+ years of experience in an analytical role. We’d love to connect with you



You excel in Excel? Great!
Have SQL/BigQuery skills? That’s what we need as well!
Work in a structured and efficient manner? That’s how we like it!
Like to connect commercial and analytical worlds together? Come and show us!

Makro Nederland is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applications from anyone who meets the above criteria.





Additional Information



Work at MAKRO

At Makro, you work in an ambitious and entrepreneurial environment where you get a lot of freedom to develop yourself. You will have the space to learn, grow and make an impact in a competitive foodservice market.







WHAT DO WE OFFER YOU?



We offer an attractive salary within transparent salary scales, supplemented with good working conditions.

* The salary range mentioned is indicative and the final offer is determined on the basis of relevant experience and skills.
A collective VGZ health insurance with interesting discounts.
An age-related pension with BE FRANK.
A bicycle plan to encourage employees to use the bicycle to go to work.
Travel allowance from 10 km in accordance with company policy. Public transport fully reimbursed.
Free parking when you come by car.
10% staff discount in the NL branches.
Investment in your talent through in-house training and a 'GoodHabitz' subscription.
Extra vacation days from the age of 41.
Lots of freedom, responsibility and career opportunities

'SAMEN MAKEN WE MAKRO'

At Makro you don't just work for the organization, but together with each other. We believe in short lines of communication, entrepreneurship and a working environment in which everyone contributes to success.



APPLICATIONS

Ready to start your career as a Pricing data analyst at Makro? Apply today via our website.



Acquisition in response to this vacancy is not appreciated.

A reference check can be part of the selection procedure.

Title: Data Engineer/ Analist
Description:
Waar ligt de grens tussen uw tuin en die van uw buurman, en welk vastgoed is van wie? Het Kadaster registreert grond en gebouwen in Nederland en de bijbehorende rechten. Met data over vastgoed en klantinteractie halen wij maximale waarde. Zet jezelf op de kaart als:

Data Engineer/ Analist

Als Data engineer/ Analist speel je een sleutelrol in het ontsluiten, structureren en beschikbaar maken van data rondom klantreizen en klantinteracties. In de eerste fase van de functie ligt de nadruk op data engineering: je werkt intensief samen met collega’s om datasets uit verschillende bronnen betrouwbaar te ontsluiten in Databricks. Je zorgt dat deze correct en consistent beschikbaar zijn in Power BI.

Je werkt samen met teams aan verbeterinitiatieven en sluit aan bij verbeterborden in Jira. Zo wordt data structureel ingezet ter ondersteuning van procesverbeteringen en continue verbetering. Zodra de datafundamenten stevig staan, verschuift de focus meer richting data‑analyse: het analyseren en interpreteren van data, het vertalen van inzichten naar concrete informatieproducten en het doen van onderbouwde aanbevelingen voor het verbeteren van klantinteracties.

Wat ga je doen?

Je werkt zowel zelfstandig als in nauwe samenwerking binnen een multidisciplinair team en weet de wensen van belanghebbenden scherp te krijgen en te vertalen naar duurzame, data‑gedreven oplossingen.


Je voert analyses uit op datasets, onderzoekt verbanden en presenteert inzichten op een beeldende en toegankelijke manier met behulp van dashboards en rapportages voor het onderdeel Klantinteractie binnen het Kadaster.
Je ontwikkelt en onderhoudt dashboards die data helder visualiseren, zowel voor periodieke dashboards en rapportage als ad-hoc vraagstukken.
Met jouw analytische vaardigheden en kritische blik geef je onderbouwde adviezen en oplossingsrichtingen voor vraagstukken rond klantreizen en binnen Klantinteractie.
Naast de technische kant van je werk, is het jouw verantwoordelijkheid om actief samen te werken met andere teams.
Je zoekt actief naar verbeteringen in data- en werkprocessen, en draagt bij aan de verdere ontwikkeling van de afdeling door optimalisaties en automatisering waar mogelijk.
Je spreekt de taal van zowel de directe collega’s binnen Klantinteractie en de collega’s van de IT-afdeling. Je fungeert als vraagbaak en sparringpartner voor collega’s op het gebied van data, dashboards, rapportages.


Ons team

Klantinteractie bestaat uit de volgende onderdelen; het Klantcontactcenter met 4 teams, het Online team en het team Zakelijke markt. Klantinteractie maakt deel uit van de directie Operatie, Dienstverlening en Registratie (ODR) waarin alle uitvoerende onderdelen van het Kadaster zijn ondergebracht.

Jouw profiel

Wij zoeken een zelfstandige en teamgerichte data engineer/analist die analytisch en communicatief sterk is, een scherp oog heeft voor detail en accuraat te werk gaat. Jij bent een zelfstarter en doorzetter. Daarnaast heb je oog voor het grotere geheel en ben je gericht op continu verbeteren. Verder breng je het volgende mee:


Een HBO-diploma en minimaal 2-3 jaar relevante werkervaring.
Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring met Python. Je hebt een streepje voor als je ervaring hebt met SQL.
Affiniteit met Aritificial Intelligence (AI), begrip van de theoretische basis, én het vermogen om AI praktisch toe te kunnen passen.
Ervaring met verschillende datavisualisatie tools zoals bijvoorbeeld in SAP SAC en Power BI. Je hebt een streepje voor als je ervaring hebt met Azure Databricks.
Sterke analytische vaardigheden en de kunst om complexe data overzichtelijk en helder te maken.
Gestructureerd in je aanpak, met oog voor zowel detail als het einddoel.
Je stelt de juiste vragen en bent gemotiveerd om de onderliggende processen van de afdeling te leren begrijpen.
Je zoekt actief naar manieren om bij te dragen aan de ontwikkeling van de organisatie, en bent continu op zoek naar verbeteringen.


Wij bieden

Jij maakt de keuzes die passen binnen een flexibele werkweek (32-36 uur). Flexibel en hybride werken staan bij ons centraal. Je bent minimaal twee dagen per week op kantoor aanwezig om samen te werken met je collega’s. Wil je meer samenwerken en vaker langskomen? Graag! Daarnaast kun je vanuit huis werken. Jouw standplaats is ons kantoor in Zwolle.

Je bruto maandsalaris is (op basis van 36 uur/ afhankelijk van kennis en ervaring) minimaal € 3.747,37 en maximaal € 5.420,31 (schaal 10). Daarnaast ontvang je maandelijks maximaal € 1.355,08 aan IKB (Individueel Keuze Budget). Het IKB bestaat uit onder andere vakantiegeld, eindejaarsuitkering en bovenwettelijk verlof. Je kiest zelf waarvoor en wanneer je het IKB wilt gebruiken.

Je hebt recht op 144 uur verlof per jaar op basis van een fulltime dienstverband. Deze vakantie-uren kun je eenvoudig uitbreiden via je IKB.

Er is altijd ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling, in overleg met je leidinggevende kun je opleidingen en cursussen volgen. Met je Persoonlijk Ontwikkel Budget investeer je in je duurzame inzetbaarheid.

 Verder bieden wij:  


een goede pensioenregeling (ABP). Een volledig overzicht van onze arbeidsvoorwaarden kun je terugvinden in de cao Kadaster.
de mogelijkheid om je sportschoolabonnement fiscaal vriendelijk uit te ruilen tot 300,- per jaar.
een tegemoetkoming voor je zorgverzekering van € 150,70 bruto per maand.  
een tegemoetkoming in je studieschuld als je tot de ‘pechgeneratie’ behoort.  
het Kadaster zet in op duurzaamheid en vitaliteit. Wij stimuleren reizen met het openbaar vervoer of met de fiets. Je ontvangt een vergoeding voor het meest groene vervoer naar je werk; een fietsvergoeding van € 0,23 per kilometer. Daarnaast ontvang je een NS business card waarmee je vrij kunt reizen door heel het land naar je werk en eventueel een kilometervergoeding voor autogebruik.

HTML:
Waar ligt de grens tussen uw tuin en die van uw buurman, en welk vastgoed is van wie? Het Kadaster registreert grond en gebouwen in Nederland en de bijbehorende rechten. Met data over vastgoed en klantinteractie halen wij maximale waarde. Zet jezelf op de kaart als:

Data Engineer/ Analist

Als Data engineer/ Analist speel je een sleutelrol in het ontsluiten, structureren en beschikbaar maken van data rondom klantreizen en klantinteracties. In de eerste fase van de functie ligt de nadruk op data engineering: je werkt intensief samen met collega’s om datasets uit verschillende bronnen betrouwbaar te ontsluiten in Databricks. Je zorgt dat deze correct en consistent beschikbaar zijn in Power BI.

Je werkt samen met teams aan verbeterinitiatieven en sluit aan bij verbeterborden in Jira. Zo wordt data structureel ingezet ter ondersteuning van procesverbeteringen en continue verbetering. Zodra de datafundamenten stevig staan, verschuift de focus meer richting data‑analyse: het analyseren en interpreteren van data, het vertalen van inzichten naar concrete informatieproducten en het doen van onderbouwde aanbevelingen voor het verbeteren van klantinteracties.

Wat ga je doen?

Je werkt zowel zelfstandig als in nauwe samenwerking binnen een multidisciplinair team en weet de wensen van belanghebbenden scherp te krijgen en te vertalen naar duurzame, data‑gedreven oplossingen.

  • Je voert analyses uit op datasets, onderzoekt verbanden en presenteert inzichten op een beeldende en toegankelijke manier met behulp van dashboards en rapportages voor het onderdeel Klantinteractie binnen het Kadaster.
  • Je ontwikkelt en onderhoudt dashboards die data helder visualiseren, zowel voor periodieke dashboards en rapportage als ad-hoc vraagstukken.
  • Met jouw analytische vaardigheden en kritische blik geef je onderbouwde adviezen en oplossingsrichtingen voor vraagstukken rond klantreizen en binnen Klantinteractie.
  • Naast de technische kant van je werk, is het jouw verantwoordelijkheid om actief samen te werken met andere teams.
  • Je zoekt actief naar verbeteringen in data- en werkprocessen, en draagt bij aan de verdere ontwikkeling van de afdeling door optimalisaties en automatisering waar mogelijk.
  • Je spreekt de taal van zowel de directe collega’s binnen Klantinteractie en de collega’s van de IT-afdeling. Je fungeert als vraagbaak en sparringpartner voor collega’s op het gebied van data, dashboards, rapportages.

Ons team

Klantinteractie bestaat uit de volgende onderdelen; het Klantcontactcenter met 4 teams, het Online team en het team Zakelijke markt. Klantinteractie maakt deel uit van de directie Operatie, Dienstverlening en Registratie (ODR) waarin alle uitvoerende onderdelen van het Kadaster zijn ondergebracht.

Jouw profiel

Wij zoeken een zelfstandige en teamgerichte data engineer/analist die analytisch en communicatief sterk is, een scherp oog heeft voor detail en accuraat te werk gaat. Jij bent een zelfstarter en doorzetter. Daarnaast heb je oog voor het grotere geheel en ben je gericht op continu verbeteren. Verder breng je het volgende mee:

  • Een HBO-diploma en minimaal 2-3 jaar relevante werkervaring.
  • Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring met Python. Je hebt een streepje voor als je ervaring hebt met SQL.
  • Affiniteit met Aritificial Intelligence (AI), begrip van de theoretische basis, én het vermogen om AI praktisch toe te kunnen passen.
  • Ervaring met verschillende datavisualisatie tools zoals bijvoorbeeld in SAP SAC en Power BI. Je hebt een streepje voor als je ervaring hebt met Azure Databricks.
  • Sterke analytische vaardigheden en de kunst om complexe data overzichtelijk en helder te maken.
  • Gestructureerd in je aanpak, met oog voor zowel detail als het einddoel.
  • Je stelt de juiste vragen en bent gemotiveerd om de onderliggende processen van de afdeling te leren begrijpen.
  • Je zoekt actief naar manieren om bij te dragen aan de ontwikkeling van de organisatie, en bent continu op zoek naar verbeteringen.

Wij bieden

Jij maakt de keuzes die passen binnen een flexibele werkweek (32-36 uur). Flexibel en hybride werken staan bij ons centraal. Je bent minimaal twee dagen per week op kantoor aanwezig om samen te werken met je collega’s. Wil je meer samenwerken en vaker langskomen? Graag! Daarnaast kun je vanuit huis werken. Jouw standplaats is ons kantoor in Zwolle.

Je bruto maandsalaris is (op basis van 36 uur/ afhankelijk van kennis en ervaring) minimaal € 3.747,37 en maximaal € 5.420,31 (schaal 10). Daarnaast ontvang je maandelijks maximaal € 1.355,08 aan IKB (Individueel Keuze Budget). Het IKB bestaat uit onder andere vakantiegeld, eindejaarsuitkering en bovenwettelijk verlof. Je kiest zelf waarvoor en wanneer je het IKB wilt gebruiken.

Je hebt recht op 144 uur verlof per jaar op basis van een fulltime dienstverband. Deze vakantie-uren kun je eenvoudig uitbreiden via je IKB.

Er is altijd ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling, in overleg met je leidinggevende kun je opleidingen en cursussen volgen. Met je Persoonlijk Ontwikkel Budget investeer je in je duurzame inzetbaarheid.

 Verder bieden wij:  

  • een goede pensioenregeling (ABP). Een volledig overzicht van onze arbeidsvoorwaarden kun je terugvinden in de cao Kadaster.
  • de mogelijkheid om je sportschoolabonnement fiscaal vriendelijk uit te ruilen tot 300,- per jaar.
  • een tegemoetkoming voor je zorgverzekering van € 150,70 bruto per maand.  
  • een tegemoetkoming in je studieschuld als je tot de ‘pechgeneratie’ behoort.  
  • het Kadaster zet in op duurzaamheid en vitaliteit. Wij stimuleren reizen met het openbaar vervoer of met de fiets. Je ontvangt een vergoeding voor het meest groene vervoer naar je werk; een fietsvergoeding van € 0,23 per kilometer. Daarnaast ontvang je een NS business card waarmee je vrij kunt reizen door heel het land naar je werk en eventueel een kilometervergoeding voor autogebruik.
  • een thuiswerkvergoeding en een vergoeding voor de inrichting van je thuiswerkplek.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analyst (Pensions)

As a preferred supplier to one of our biggest financial organisations, I am seeking for a Data Analyst for a 12 months+ contract in Netherlands.

Responsibilities:

For a data management team within the pension domain, we are looking for a senior Data Analyst who will play a key role in the further development and maturity of the data landscape and the team. You are able to translate complex research questions into efficient and correct data analyses, supported by existing data models.

As a senior analyst, you work independently within a multidisciplinary scrum team and act as a substantive sparring partner for stakeholders. In addition, you supervise junior analysts and monitor the quality of delivered analyses. The environment is characterized by complex data models, high quality requirements and a strong focus on data-driven working.

Experience:

• HBO/WO working and thinking level

• At least 5 years of experience as a Data Analyst within the pension domain

• Demonstrable experience with complex data models and data analyses

• Strong analytical skills and the ability to quickly master new concepts

• Experience with SQL (PL/SQL) and Python

• Experience with Agile/Scrum working

• Strong communication skills and stakeholder management

• Experience in supervising junior colleagues and reviewing work

• Able to operate independently within a complex environment

Please apply with an updated CV, if interested or looking for a new opportunity as soon as possible.

Title: BI & Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role

Geely EU IT & Digital is looking for an experienced BI & Data Analyst to join our growing team. You will be responsible for designing and maintaining Power BI dashboards and reports that support business decision-making across Sales, Aftersales, and Operations. In addition to reporting, you will play a foundational role in building our data warehouse infrastructure — helping to establish the data architecture that will power analytics at scale across the European market.

Key Responsibilities


Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports for stakeholders across Sales, Aftersales, Finance, and Supply Chain.
Translate complex business requirements into clear, actionable data visualizations and KPI tracking frameworks.
Participate in data warehouse planning and preparation: contribute to data modeling, define source-to-target mappings, and document data lineage.
Work with data engineers and IT teams to ensure data quality, consistency, and governance across systems (ERP, DMS, TMS, WMS, etc.).
Monitor and analyze automotive business metrics including vehicle sales performance, spare parts inventory, dealer network KPIs, and aftersales revenue.
Write and optimize SQL queries for data extraction, transformation, and ad-hoc analysis.
Support business users with self-service BI capabilities, including training and documentation.


Requirements

Technical Skills


3+ years of hands-on experience with Microsoft Power BI, including DAX, Power Query (M language), and report deployment via Power BI Service.
Solid proficiency in SQL; experience with SQL Server, PostgreSQL, or Azure SQL.
Foundational knowledge of data warehousing concepts: dimensional modeling (star/snowflake schema), ETL/ELT pipelines, and data lake architecture.
Familiarity with cloud data warehouse platforms such as AWS (Redshift, Glue, S3), Databricks, Azure Synapse, or Snowflake; hands-on experience with AWS or Databricks is a strong plus.


Domain Knowledge


Automotive industry experience is strongly preferred — particularly in vehicle sales, aftersales / spare parts, dealer management, or supply chain.
Understanding of automotive business systems and data sources: DMS (Dealer Management System), ERP, WMS, TMS.
Ability to interpret business KPIs such as order fill rate, parts turnover, and dealer network performance.


Soft Skills & Other


Strong communication skills — able to present data insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Proactive and structured thinker; comfortable working in a fast-paced, international environment.
Fluent in English; Chinese language skills are a strong advantage given cross-team collaboration with HQ.


NICE TO HAVE


Python / pandas for data processing and automation.
dbt (data build tool) for transformation pipelines.
Azure Data Factory or similar orchestration tools.
Experience working with EV or Chinese automotive brands.
Familiarity with SAP or Oracle ERP systems.
Agile / Scrum project delivery methodology.


What We Offer


Competitive remuneration package.
A fast-paced dynamic environment in a successful international organization.
International exposure with the chance to adapt locally and make a difference.
Growth potential.


Geely’s mission is to create a smart mobility experience that exceeds user expectations, become a tech-driven global auto company and the most competitive and respectable Chinese auto brand in the world.

Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Product Data Analyst based in Netherlands.

This is a high-impact product analytics role embedded directly within a product team that is already highly data-literate and technically strong. You will act as the dedicated analytics partner to product managers, helping them go deeper into user behavior, activation, and adoption patterns that drive product success. The role combines rigorous analytical depth with strong product thinking, enabling you to influence roadmap decisions, improve user experience, and uncover insights that go beyond standard reporting. Working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment, you will contribute to both strategic direction and hands-on experimentation while shaping how users derive value from a leading workflow automation platform. This is an opportunity to join a highly technical, product-led organization where your insights will directly impact product growth and user engagement.

Accountabilities


Act as the dedicated analytics partner to product managers, supporting deep analysis of product usage, user behavior, and feature performance.
Conduct advanced product analyses across activation, onboarding, adoption, retention, and expansion to identify key behavioral drivers.
Deliver cross-product and segment-level insights that provide a broader perspective beyond individual product squads.
Co-develop hypotheses with product managers and design analytical approaches to validate product decisions.
Influence product roadmap decisions by providing clear, data-backed recommendations on what to build, improve, or deprioritize.
Design, evaluate, and interpret A/B tests and experiments, ensuring statistical rigor and actionable outcomes.
Partner with engineering and product teams on event instrumentation, tracking design, and data quality improvements.
Identify opportunities for deeper analysis proactively, surfacing insights and recommendations even without formal requests.
Translate complex data findings into clear, structured narratives that support decision-making at all levels.
Contribute to improving analytical standards, methodologies, and experimentation practices across the product organization.


Requirements


5+ years of experience in product analytics, data analysis, or related roles within B2B SaaS environments, ideally in product-led growth companies.
Strong experience analyzing user behavior, product funnels, activation metrics, retention drivers, and feature adoption.
Deep expertise in SQL and strong familiarity with modern data stacks (e.g., BigQuery, dbt, Metabase or equivalent tools).
Strong understanding of statistical methods, including hypothesis testing, bias detection, and experimental design.
Proven experience designing, running, and interpreting A/B tests and product experiments.
Experience defining event tracking schemas and collaborating with engineering teams on instrumentation.
Strong ability to independently identify high-impact analytical opportunities and drive them to completion.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to turn complex analysis into clear, decision-ready insights.
Strong product intuition and ability to influence roadmap decisions with data-backed arguments.
Experience with behavioral segmentation, cohort analysis, and advanced retention modeling.
Python experience for data analysis is a plus (e.g., pandas, scipy, statsmodels).
Familiarity with causal inference methods or experimentation platforms (e.g., GrowthBook, Statsig, Eppo) is an advantage.
Experience working with technical or developer-focused products is a strong plus.


Benefits


Competitive compensation package aligned with market standards.
Equity participation, giving you ownership in the company’s success.
Fully remote-first environment with flexibility across Europe.
Generous vacation allowance depending on location (up to 30 days in Europe).
Annual learning and development budget for courses, books, events, or coaching.
Unlimited AI tooling budget to support productivity and innovation.
Strong culture of transparency, autonomy, and high ownership.
Regular hackathons and collaborative innovation initiatives.
Opportunity to work closely with highly technical product teams in a data-driven environment.
Exposure to one of Europe’s fastest-growing open-source and automation platforms.
Remote-friendly culture with regular team offsites and global collaboration opportunities.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
