Title: Systemintegrator
Description:
Über Carrier

Carrier Global Corporation, ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen für intelligente Klima- und Energielösungen, engagiert sich für Innovationen, die Komfort, Sicherheit und Nachhaltigkeit in den Alltag bringen. Durch modernste Entwicklungen in Bereichen wie Temperaturregelung, Luftqualität und Transport verbessern wir das Leben der Menschen, unterstützen kritische Branchen und gewährleisten den sicheren Transport von Lebensmitteln, lebensrettenden Medikamenten und mehr. Seit der Erfindung der modernen Klimaanlage im Jahr 1902 verfolgen wir ein klares Ziel: das Leben der Menschen und unsere gemeinsame Welt zu verbessern. Unsere führende Position verdanken wir unserer erstklassigen, inklusiven Belegschaft, die den Kunden in den Mittelpunkt stellt. Weitere Informationen finden Sie unter corporate.carrier.com oder folgen Sie Carrier in sozialen Medien @Carrier.

Automated Logic ist ein führender Hersteller innovativer Lösungen im Bereich der Gebäudeautomation. Unsere Systeme kommen vor allem in großen Industrieanlagen sowie in hochsensiblen Umgebungen wie Rechenzentren zum Einsatz – dort, wo Zuverlässigkeit, Effizienz und Transparenz von zentraler Bedeutung sind.

Als Teil der Carrier Gruppe profitieren wir von der Stärke und Tradition eines weltweit agierenden Technologieunternehmens. Carrier entwickelt, produziert und vermarktet Lösungen für den Wohn-, Dienstleistungs-, Gesundheits- und Industriesektor. Im Fokus stehen stets die Optimierung des Energieverbrauchs, die Verbesserung der Luftqualität sowie die Steigerung des Komforts in Gebäuden.

Carrier gilt als Erfinder der modernen Klimatechnik und ist heute global Marktführer im Bereich Kälte- und Klimatechnik. Seit der Gründung im Jahr 1902 steht das Unternehmen für kontinuierliche Innovation und Spitzentechnologie. Diese Werte prägen auch den Charakter von Automated Logic: technisch präzise, offen und vernetzt in der Systemarchitektur, nachhaltig im Denken und kompromisslos zuverlässig im Betrieb.

Wir bieten unseren Kunden ein breites Spektrum an Produkten und Lösungen – immer mit dem Anspruch, Gebäude intelligenter, effizienter und lebenswerter zu machen.

Inzwischen arbeiten für uns 54.400 Mitarbeiter, die einen Umsatz von 18,9 Mrd. US$ generieren.

Zur Verstärkung unseres Servicetechniker Teams in Frankfurt, suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt eine/einen Systemintegrator MSR (m/w/d).

Ihre Aufgaben


Programmierung / Parametrierung von DDC- und GLT-Anlagen
Inbetriebnahme von DDC-Anlagen und Bussystemen (BACnet, Modbus etc.)
Instandhaltung von Gebäudeautomatisierungsanlagen
Dokumentation der Ergebnisse in Funktions- und Leistungsbeschreibungen.
Behebung von Störungen an Anlagen und Maschinen
Teilnahme an der rotierenden Rufbereitschaft
Durchführung von Wartungen an Anlagen und Leitsystemen beim Kunden


Ihr Profil


Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung als Elektroniker/Mechatroniker oder ähnliches
Qualifizierter Abschluß zur Elektrofachkraft
Berufserfahrung auf dem Gebiet der Gebäudeautomatisierung
Eigenverantwortliches Handeln sowie Teamfähigkeit
Hohes Maß an Eigenverantwortung, Flexibilität und Belastbarkeit
Selbstständiger und lösungsorientierter Arbeitsstil
Flexibilität und Reisebereitschaft
Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
Freundliches und kommunikatives Auftreten


Wir Bieten


Einen sichereren, unbefristeten Arbeitsplatz in einem international agierenden Unternehmen einer zukunftssicheren Branche, die durch Wachstum geprägt ist.
Ein hochprofessionelles Arbeitsumfeld mit anspruchsvollen Aufgaben und einem attraktivem Vergütungspaket
Vertraglich zugesicherte Sonderzahlungen wie z.B. bei Jubiläum, Mitarbeiterempfehlung, Heirat oder Geburt
30 Tage Urlaub sowie zusätzliche freie Tage für besondere Anlässe
Firmenwagen mit individueller Konfigurationsmöglichkeit und Privatnutzung (Tankkartennutzung auch während des Urlaubs)
Private Unfallversicherung durch Carrier (weltweit 24/7)
Teilnahme am Carrier Weiterbildungsprogramm, d.h. Kostenübernahme bei Fortbildungsmaßnahmen zur persönlichen und fachlichen Weiterentwicklung wie z.B. Meister, Betriebswirt usw.
Internationale Karrieremöglichkeiten durch Konzernstruktur
Green Benefits wie z.B. Jobrad als zusätzliches Mobilitätsangebot
Gesundheitsmanagement mit Vorsorgeuntersuchungsangeboten und optimierten Arbeitsplätzen
Familienfreundliche Personalpolitik zur Unterstützung in verschiedenen Lebensphasen
Exklusive Mitarbeiterangebote mit hochwertigen Sonderkonditionen für Carrier Produkte aber auch Leistungen anderer renommierter Hersteller und Marken.
Coffee & Tea Flatrate sowie Wasserspender
Meet & Greets; Mitarbeiter- & Team Events
Und als i-Tüpfelchen, großartige Kollegen und Kolleginnen


Vergütungsspanne

Die jährliche Vergütung für diese Position liegt zwischen €50,500.00 - €101,500.00 jährlich. Faktoren wie Fähigkeiten, Ausbildung, Erfahrung und weitere Qualifikationen können die Vergütung innerhalb dieser Spanne beeinflussen.

Weitere Vergütung

Diese Position kann Anspruch auf kurzfristige Bonuszahlungen haben.

Leistungen

Wir bieten ein wettbewerbsfähiges Gesamtvergütungspaket mit zusätzlichen Benefits und Gesundheitsprogrammen. Diese variieren je nach Position und Standort.

Gleichbehandlung und Nichtdiskriminierung

Carrier verpflichtet sich zur Gleichbehandlung.

Alle qualifizierten Bewerber werden unabhängig von persönlichen Merkmalen berücksichtigt.

Bei Bedarf an Unterstützung im Bewerbungsprozess kontaktieren Sie uns unter Carrier.Recruiting@carrier.com

Bewerbungsfrist

Mindestens 14 Tage ab Veröffentlichungsdatum: 8 June 2026

Datenschutzhinweis für Bewerber

Bitte klicken Sie auf den folgenden Link.

Einsatz von KI

Technologiegestützte Tools können einzelne Schritte des Einstellungsprozesses unterstützen, bleiben dabei jedoch stets unter menschlicher Aufsicht.

Title: Bachelor of Science (DHBW) Data Science und Künstliche Intelligenz (m/w/d) Standort Winnenden 2027
Description:
Das Werden Deine WOW-Momente


Wir schaffen mit dir gemeinsam eine solide Basis für deinen beruflichen Einstieg mit der Möglichkeit, wichtige Theorie und spannende Aufgaben in der Praxis vereinen zu können.
An der Dualen Hochschule Baden-Württemberg in Heilbronn werden neben grundlegenden IT-Kenntnissen auch Methoden für die Datenanalysen und zur Interpretation großer Datenmengen vermittelt.
Erlange fundierte Kenntnisse und Fähigkeiten im Bereich der Künstlichen Intelligenz, um komplexe Probleme zu lösen und innovative Lösungen zu entwickeln.
Parallel zur Theorie steigst du direkt in die Praxis bei Kärcher ein. Du optimierst komplexe Geschäftsprozesse, betreust moderne Informationssysteme und setzt dein theoretisches Wissen bei aktuellen Projekten um.


Es Wäre WOW, Wenn Du Das Hier Mitbringst


Wir suchen kluge Köpfe, die mit Fleiß und Engagement jeden Tag Spitzenleistungen bringen und die Chance ergreifen möchten, akademisches Wissen und praktische Erfahrungen direkt in Einklang zu bringen.
Du gehst deinen Weg voller Selbstvertrauen, hast einen erfolgreichen Abschluss der allgemeinen Hochschulreife in der Tasche und überzeugst uns mit (sehr) guten Noten in Mathematik sowie Informatik.
Dich zeichnet ein hohes Maß an Lernbereitschaft, Belastbarkeit und Zuverlässigkeit aus.
Kommunikationsfähigkeit, Aufgeschlossenheit und Spaß an der Arbeit im Team gehören zu deinen persönlichen Stärken.
Gute Englischkenntnisse und grundlegende Programmierkenntnisse sind wünschenswert.


Unser Kärcher WOW-Paket


Eine Übernahmegarantie mit einem unbefristeten Arbeitsvertrag am Ende deines dualen Studiums. Das gibt dir Planungssicherheit für deine berufliche Zukunft.
Ein Kennenlernnachmittag vor deinem dualen Studium und ein Kick-Off-Seminar zu Beginn deines dualen Studiums, damit du dich schnell bei uns zurechtfindest und die anderen Auszubildenden und Studierenden kennenlernen kannst.
Freue dich über eine attraktive Ausbildungsvergütung (1. Jahr: 1.445 €, 2. Jahr: 1.629 €, 3. Jahr: 1.866 €), einen eigenen Laptop während des kompletten Studiums und ein kostenloses Deutschlandticket JugendBW.
Zu deinen 30 Tagen Urlaub pro Studienjahr erhältst du zusätzlich zwei freie Tage, die du auch jahresübergreifend nehmen kannst. Darüber hinaus sind Weihnachten und Silvester arbeitsfrei.
Freue dich auf ein spannendes Praxissemester in einer unserer Auslandsgesellschaften, das wir finanziell unterstützen. Das genügt dir noch nicht? Dann hast du die Möglichkeit, auf eigene Faust ein Theoriesemester im Ausland zu verbringen.
Durch eine 35-Stunden-Woche, Gleitzeit und die Möglichkeit, auch mal mobil zu arbeiten, kannst du deinen Arbeitstag weitestgehend flexibel gestalten.


Also: Wanna WOW with us?

Lade einfach deinen Lebenslauf und dein Anschreiben sowie deine letzten beiden Zeugnisse online hoch und hinterlasse deine Kontaktinfos. Wir freuen uns darauf, dich von deiner persönlichen WOW-Seite kennenzulernen! Wir begrüßen die Bewerbung von Menschen mit Behinderung, deren Integration uns ein besonderes Anliegen ist.

Together towards a clean world.

Sandra Eitle

youngstars@karcher.com | +49 151 1169 1325

  • Du gehst deinen Weg voller Selbstvertrauen, hast einen erfolgreichen Abschluss der allgemeinen Hochschulreife in der Tasche und überzeugst uns mit (sehr) guten Noten in Mathematik sowie Informatik.
  • Dich zeichnet ein hohes Maß an Lernbereitschaft, Belastbarkeit und Zuverlässigkeit aus.
  • Kommunikationsfähigkeit, Aufgeschlossenheit und Spaß an der Arbeit im Team gehören zu deinen persönlichen Stärken.
  • Gute Englischkenntnisse und grundlegende Programmierkenntnisse sind wünschenswert.

Unser Kärcher WOW-Paket

  • Eine Übernahmegarantie mit einem unbefristeten Arbeitsvertrag am Ende deines dualen Studiums. Das gibt dir Planungssicherheit für deine berufliche Zukunft.
  • Ein Kennenlernnachmittag vor deinem dualen Studium und ein Kick-Off-Seminar zu Beginn deines dualen Studiums, damit du dich schnell bei uns zurechtfindest und die anderen Auszubildenden und Studierenden kennenlernen kannst.
  • Freue dich über eine attraktive Ausbildungsvergütung (1. Jahr: 1.445 €, 2. Jahr: 1.629 €, 3. Jahr: 1.866 €), einen eigenen Laptop während des kompletten Studiums und ein kostenloses Deutschlandticket JugendBW.
  • Zu deinen 30 Tagen Urlaub pro Studienjahr erhältst du zusätzlich zwei freie Tage, die du auch jahresübergreifend nehmen kannst. Darüber hinaus sind Weihnachten und Silvester arbeitsfrei.
  • Freue dich auf ein spannendes Praxissemester in einer unserer Auslandsgesellschaften, das wir finanziell unterstützen. Das genügt dir noch nicht? Dann hast du die Möglichkeit, auf eigene Faust ein Theoriesemester im Ausland zu verbringen.
  • Durch eine 35-Stunden-Woche, Gleitzeit und die Möglichkeit, auch mal mobil zu arbeiten, kannst du deinen Arbeitstag weitestgehend flexibel gestalten.

Also: Wanna WOW with us?

Lade einfach deinen Lebenslauf und dein Anschreiben sowie deine letzten beiden Zeugnisse online hoch und hinterlasse deine Kontaktinfos. Wir freuen uns darauf, dich von deiner persönlichen WOW-Seite kennenzulernen! Wir begrüßen die Bewerbung von Menschen mit Behinderung, deren Integration uns ein besonderes Anliegen ist.

Together towards a clean world.

Sandra Eitle

youngstars@karcher.com | +49 151 1169 1325

Title: Installationstechniker (m/w/d) – Sicherheitstechnik
Description:
Unternehmensbeschreibung

Zur Verstärkung unseres Expertenteams suchen wir ab sofort einen Installationstechniker (m/w/d) für Sicherheitstechnik.

Von der Planung über die Installation bis hin zur Inbetriebnahme und Wartung – unsere Expertinnen und Experten der Securitas Technology bieten den Rundum-Service für modernste und individuelle Sicherheitslösungen.

Einsätze im eigenen Bundesland, je nach Projekt wenige Tage bis ca. vier Wochen

Montag–Freitag, keine Wochenendarbeit, keine Rufbereitschaft

Start und Ende zu Hause – Fahrtzeit zählt als Arbeitszeit

‍ Einarbeitung durch erfahrene Kolleg:innen im laufenden Betrieb

Moderne Ausstattung – inkl. Fahrzeug, Tablet, Smartphone & Werkzeug

Stellenbeschreibung

Als Installationstechniker (m/w/d) für Sicherheitstechnik übernehmen Sie die Installation, Inbetriebnahme und Übergabe moderner Gefahrenmeldesysteme – zum Beispiel Einbruch- oder Brandmeldeanlagen – bei unseren Kunden vor Ort.

Dabei arbeiten Sie projektbezogen, eigenverantwortlich und im engen Austausch mit der Projektleitung.

Ihre Aufgaben im Überblick:


Installation und Programmierung sicherheitstechnischer Anlagen
Durchführung von Funktionsprüfungen und Abnahmen
Anmeldung der Systeme bei Behörden (Feuerwehr, Polizei)
Dokumentation und Kommunikation über digitale Endgeräte (Tablet, Laptop)
Projektbezogene Arbeitseinsätze – in einem Team oder mit Partnergewerken
Teilnahme an Briefings, regelmäßiger Austausch mit der Projektleitung


Qualifikationen

Das bringen Sie mit:

Muss-Kriterien


Abgeschlossene elektrotechnische Ausbildung (z. B. Elektroniker:in, Elektroinstallateur:in)
Führerschein Klasse B
Deutschkenntnisse auf mindestens B2-Niveau


Das bringen Sie idealerweise zusätzlich mit


Berufserfahrung in der Installation sicherheitstechnischer Systeme (z. B. EMA/BMA)
Herstellerzertifikate oder spezifische Schulungen im Bereich Sicherheitstechnik
Selbstständige und strukturierte Arbeitsweise
Ausgeprägte Serviceorientierung
Motivation zur fachlichen Weiterentwicklung


Zusätzliche Informationen

Ihre Benefits bei Securitas:


Top bezahlt – Jahresgehalt von 35.000 bis 50.000 € brutto, abhängig von der Erfahrung
Berufliche Sicherheit – Dank eines unbefristeten Arbeitsvertrags beim Marktführer
Voll erholt – Dank 30 Urlaubstagen pro Jahr
Immer mobil – mit Ihrem Securitas Dienstfahrzeug, inkl. Tankkarte und 365 Tage Privatnutzung
Wertgeschätzt – durch steuerfreie Gutscheinprämien zu Geburtstag, Hochzeit oder Weihnachten und vielen weiteren Anlässen
Clever gespart – mit attraktiven Mitarbeiterrabatten bei z.B. Pay-TV Anbieter, Mobilfunk, Fitnessstudios, Banken und zahlreichen weiteren Anbietern
Gut gerüstet – Dank der zusätzlichen betrieblichen Altersvorsorge (bAV) mit bis zu 20% Arbeitgeberzuschuss
Immer geschützt – mit der kostenlosen Securitas Gruppen-Unfallversicherung – auch in Ihrer Freizeit
Gut geschult – Dank des umfangreichen Securitas Seminarkatalogs sowie der Hersteller- und produktspezifischen Technik-Schulungen
Rundum beraten – mit festen Ansprechpartner:innen in der Projektleitung
Immer informiert – mit dem Securitas Online-Mitarbeiterportal

Hinweis: Du erklärst dich mit deiner Einreichung der Bewerbung bereit, dass deine Bewerbung innerhalb der Securitas-Gruppe Deutschland sowie Kooperationspartner weitergeleitet wird. Dadurch erhöhen sich deine Bewerbungschancen. Wenn du dies nicht wünschst, teile uns dies bitte in deinem Schreiben mit.

Title: Lead Expert Data & GenAI (m/w/d)*
Description:
Warum zu uns

Empower People. Create Success. Bei Tagueri stehen die Mitarbeitenden und das Miteinander im Fokus. Wir brennen für unsere Themen, bringen uns proaktiv ein und geben fachlich und persönlich tagtäglich unser Bestes. Gemeinsam feiern wir unsere kleinen und großen Erfolge, freuen uns aufrichtig für- und miteinander und unterstützen uns gegenseitig. Schaffe einen echten Mehrwert und nutze die vielfältigen Möglichkeiten, Deine Kenntnisse einzubringen und zu vertiefen, eigenverantwortlich Deine Ideen und Projekte voranzutreiben sowie andere zu inspirieren und zu motivieren.

Was wir gemeinsam vorhaben


Übernimm fachliche Verantwortung: Bring deine Erfahrung ein, Themen zu treiben, Standards zu definieren und als Ansprechpartner für komplexe Fragestellungen zu agieren.
Verbinde Technologie mit Business: Verstehe, technische Themen bei Kunden zu positionieren und in konkrete Mehrwerte zu übersetzen.
Bring fundierte Data-Engineering-Expertise mit: Beherrsche moderne Datenarchitekturen sowie den produktiven Einsatz von Python (z. B. PySpark, FastAPI) und SQL.
Setze deine Kenntnisse in Cloud- und Plattformkonzepten ein: Nutze deine Erfahrung mit Azure sowie dein Verständnis von Data-Lake-Architekturen und AWS-Grundlagen.
Setze Observability gezielt ein: Nutze dein Know-how in Logging, Monitoring und Tracing zur Sicherstellung stabiler Systeme.
Beherrsche moderne AI-Methoden: Setze Generative AI, insbesondere RAG, LLM-Pipelines, Prompt Engineering und Evaluationsmethoden, zielgerichtet ein.
Denke strategisch und skalierend: Verstehe, wie sich Themen, Methoden und Wissen nachhaltig im Unternehmen und bei Kunden etablieren lassen.
Stakeholder Management: Kommuniziere (intern als auch extern) und arbeite eng mit Projektteams, Stakeholdern und Kunden zusammen.


Womit Du überzeugst


Du verfügst über mehrjährige relevante Erfahrung in IT‑/Cloud‑Security, AI/Data Engineering oder Enterprise‑Architektur.
Du hast Erfahrung in fachlicher Führung und der Skalierung von Methoden, Standards und Wissen.
Dein tiefes Verständnis moderner Datenarchitekturen und skalierbarer Datenplattformen ermöglicht Dir, komplexe Lösungen ganzheitlich zu gestalten.
Deine fundierte Erfahrung mit Apache Spark (Execution, Scheduling, Performance-Tuning) unterstützt Dich bei der Optimierung performanter Datenlösungen.
Du hast Praxis in Cloud-Umgebungen, insbesondere in Azure sowie ein gutes Verständnis von Data-Lake- und Storage-Architekturen (AWS-Grundlagen von Vorteil).
Deine Kenntnisse in Databricks sind von Vorteil, aber keine Voraussetzung.
Du arbeitest strukturiert und analytisch, kommunizierst sicher und übernimmst Verantwortung mit hoher Eigeninitiative.
Du verfügst über sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift.
Deine Englischkenntnisse sind gut in Wort und Schrift.
Du bist bereit für regelmäßige Dienstreisen innerhalb Deutschlands. Darunter verstehen wir die grundsätzliche Flexibilität unter der Woche beim Kunden vor Ort zu sein.


Worauf Du Dich freuen kannst

Unsere Besondere Unternehmenskultur


Herzliches und wertschätzendes Miteinander, in dem jede Persönlichkeit zählt
Offener Austausch und offene Türen - vom Junior bis zum Vorstand
Zahlreiche Team- und Unternehmensevents, wie unser jährliches Sommerfest zusammen mit unseren Familien


Unsere Benefits für Wohlergehen und Wachstum:


Smartes Arbeiten, z.B. mobil und mit flexiblen Arbeitszeitmodellen, Möglichkeit für Sabbaticals und Workation
Zusatzleistungen, z.B. Corporate Benefits, attraktive betriebliche Altersvorsorge und JobRad
Health Care Benefits, z.B. vergünstigte Mitgliedschaften für Urban Sports Club


Unser gemeinsamer Weg:


Intensive Einarbeitung durch ein umfangreiches Onboarding und Patenprogramm
Vielfältige interne Weiterbildungsangebote und externe Fördermaßnahmen
Internationales Austauschprogramm zur Förderung der persönlichen und beruflichen Weiterentwicklung innerhalb der Unternehmensgruppe


Und vieles mehr.

Was Du wissen solltest

Wir suchen Persönlichkeiten! Daher prüfen wir Deine Bewerbung nicht ausschließlich auf eine bestimmte Stelle bezogen, sondern als eine Bewerbung an das Unternehmen Tagueri. In einem persönlichen Gespräch finden wir gemeinsam heraus, ob Deine Ziele und unsere Vorstellungen zueinander passen.

Du findest Dich im Themengebiet und Standort nicht wieder? Sende uns gerne Deine Unterlagen zu – wir berücksichtigen Dich gerne für weitere Projekte an auch an anderen Standorten.

Alles Weitere erzählen wir Dir in einem persönlichen Gespräch. Sende Deine Unterlagen entweder per E-Mail an bewerbung@tagueri.com oder nutze den "Bewerben"-Button (Dateigröße max. 35 MB).

Über uns

Wir bei Scalian Germany sind Brückenbauer zwischen strategischer Unternehmensberatung und operativer Umsetzung. Mit rund 600 Mitarbeitenden sind wir deutschlandweit im Einsatz. Zu uns gehört auch der Energiespezialist con|energy consult gmbh. Mit unseren praxisbezogenen Beratungsmethoden und fundierter Technologieexpertise unterstützen wir namhafte Unternehmen aus den Branchen Automotive, Energie, Luft- und Raumfahrt, Bahn und Verteidigung. Die Herausforderungen unserer Kunden sehen wir als Chance, gemeinsam innovative und zukunftsfähige Lösungen im Technologie-, Projekt- und Prozessmanagement zu schaffen.


Wir bekennen uns zu den Grundsätzen der Gleichbehandlung und Nichtdiskriminierung. Die Vielfalt unserer Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter in Bezug auf Geschlecht, Hautfarbe, Alter, Herkunft, persönliche Interessen, Religion, sexuelle Orientierung und Geschlechtsidentität betrachten wir als Bereicherung. Diskriminierendes Verhalten wird von uns nicht toleriert. Dieses Bekenntnis zu Vielfalt und Inklusion haben wir durch die Unterzeichnung der Charta der Vielfalt bekräftigt.

8a und
47 SGB VIII/KJSG

Eine Ausbildung zum Erzieher/ Kinderpfleger mit staatlicher Anerkennung (m/w/d) oder ein/e abgeschlossene/s Ausbildung/Studium gemäß
7 KitaG (Kindheitspädagogen, Sozialpädagogen, Heilpädagogen, Heilerziehungspfleger, u.a.)
Einen professionellen und wertschätzenden Umgang mit Kindern und Eltern
Teamfähigkeit, Flexibilität und Bereitschaft zur Weiterentwicklung
Liebe und Leidenschaft zum Beruf

motivierte und engagierte KollegInnen

unbefristete Vollzeitstelle im Elementarbereich

ansprechende räumliche und materielle Ausstattung der Kindertageseinrichtungen

Title: Databricks & Agentic AI Expert (m/w/d)*
Description:
Warum zu uns

Empower People. Create Success. Bei Scalian Germany stehen die Mitarbeitenden und das Miteinander im Fokus. Wir brennen für unsere Themen, bringen uns proaktiv ein und geben fachlich und persönlich tagtäglich unser Bestes. Gemeinsam feiern wir unsere kleinen und großen Erfolge, freuen uns aufrichtig für- und miteinander und unterstützen uns gegenseitig. Schaffe einen echten Mehrwert und nutze die vielfältigen Möglichkeiten, Deine Kenntnisse einzubringen und zu vertiefen, eigenverantwortlich Deine Ideen und Projekte voranzutreiben sowie andere zu inspirieren und zu motivieren.

Was wir gemeinsam vorhaben


Gestalte moderne Daten- und AI-Plattformen: Entwickle skalierbare Datenarchitekturen und bringst innovative GenAI-Use-Cases in produktive Anwendungen.
Baue leistungsfähige Datenplattformen auf: Konzipiere und implementiere End-to-End Datenarchitekturen von Ingestion über Processing bis zum Serving.
Nutze moderne Data-Technologien: Arbeite mit Databricks, Apache Spark, Delta Lake und MLflow und entwickle robuste Datenpipelines.
Optimiere Datenverarbeitung und Performance: Analysiere und verbessere Spark-Workloads hinsichtlich Execution Model, Scheduling und Performance.
Setze Cloud-Architekturen um: Entwickle Lösungen auf Azure und bringe Dein Know-how in Storage-Architekturen und Data Lake Patterns ein.
Implementiere DevOps- und Deployment-Prozesse: Automatisiere Infrastruktur und Deployments mit Terraform, Bicep und CI/CD Pipelines in Azure DevOps.
Integriere Generative AI in Datenplattformen: Entwickle RAG-basierte Lösungen, LLM-Pipelines und optimierst Prompts sowie Evaluationsmethoden.
Stärke Governance und Datenstrukturen: Setze moderne Governance-Konzepte um und gestalte Datenzugriffe, Rollenmodelle und Data Domains.
Stakeholder Management: Kommuniziere (intern als auch extern) und arbeite eng mit Projektteams, Stakeholdern und Kunden zusammen.


Womit Du überzeugst


Deine Erfahrung mit Databricks, Apache Spark, Delta Lake und MLflow zeichnet Dich aus.
Du entwickelst sicher mit Python und SQL und arbeitest mit modernen DevOps-Tools.
Du bringst Erfahrung in Azure und Grundlagen in AWS mit.
Du setzt Monitoring- und Logging-Lösungen wie Application Insights ein und kennst gängige Observability-Konzepte.
Du hast Erfahrung mit RAG, LLM-Pipelines, Prompt Engineering und Evaluationsansätzen wie RAGAS.
Du verstehst Datenplattformen in ihrer Gesamtheit – von Governance über Security bis zur Skalierung.
Du verfügst über sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift.
Deine Englischkenntnisse sind gut in Wort und Schrift.
Du bist bereit für regelmäßige Dienstreisen innerhalb Deutschlands. Darunter verstehen wir die grundsätzliche Flexibilität unter der Woche beim Kunden vor Ort zu sein.

  • Intensive Einarbeitung durch ein umfangreiches Onboarding und Patenprogramm
  • Vielfältige interne Weiterbildungsangebote und externe Fördermaßnahmen
  • Internationales Austauschprogramm zur Förderung der persönlichen und beruflichen Weiterentwicklung innerhalb der Unternehmensgruppe

Und vieles mehr.

Was Du wissen solltest

Wir suchen Persönlichkeiten! Daher prüfen wir Deine Bewerbung nicht ausschließlich auf eine bestimmte Stelle bezogen, sondern als eine Bewerbung an das Unternehmen Scalian Germany. In einem persönlichen Gespräch finden wir gemeinsam heraus, ob Deine Ziele und unsere Vorstellungen zueinander passen.

Du findest Dich im Themengebiet und Standort nicht wieder? Sende uns gerne Deine Unterlagen zu – wir berücksichtigen Dich gerne für weitere Projekte an auch an anderen Standorten.

Alles Weitere erzählen wir Dir in einem persönlichen Gespräch. Sende Deine Unterlagen entweder per E-Mail an bewerbung@tagueri.com oder nutze den "Bewerben"-Button (Dateigröße max. 35 MB).

Über uns

Wir bei Scalian Germany sind Brückenbauer zwischen strategischer Unternehmensberatung und operativer Umsetzung. Mit rund 600 Mitarbeitenden sind wir deutschlandweit im Einsatz. Zu uns gehört auch der Energiespezialist con|energy consult gmbh. Mit unseren praxisbezogenen Beratungsmethoden und fundierter Technologieexpertise unterstützen wir namhafte Unternehmen aus den Branchen Automotive, Energie, Luft- und Raumfahrt, Bahn und Verteidigung. Die Herausforderungen unserer Kunden sehen wir als Chance, gemeinsam innovative und zukunftsfähige Lösungen im Technologie-, Projekt- und Prozessmanagement zu schaffen.

  • Wir bekennen uns zu den Grundsätzen der Gleichbehandlung und Nichtdiskriminierung. Die Vielfalt unserer Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter in Bezug auf Geschlecht, Hautfarbe, Alter, Herkunft, persönliche Interessen, Religion, sexuelle Orientierung und Geschlechtsidentität betrachten wir als Bereicherung. Diskriminierendes Verhalten wird von uns nicht toleriert. Dieses Bekenntnis zu Vielfalt und Inklusion haben wir durch die Unterzeichnung der Charta der Vielfalt bekräftigt.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Industry: Designer toys and collectible pieces

Location: London, Canary Wharf

Job type: Full time Permanent




About POP MART




POP MART (09992.HK) is a leading global company in the trend culture and entertainment industry. Centered around IP, POP MART has built a comprehensive platform for creative incubation and IP operation, empowering global creators while delivering exciting products, services and immersive entertainment experiences to consumers.




POP MART identifies and nurtures emerging artists and designers worldwide, creating popular character IPs through a well-established development and operation system. Our portfolio includes iconic characters such as MOLLY, SKULLPANDA, DIMOO, THE MONSTERS and Hirono. POP MART operates over 500 physical stores and more than 2,300 Robo Shops across more than thirty countries and regions, and through multiple cross border e commerce platforms reaches audiences in over ninety countries and regions, bringing joy to young consumers around the world.




POP MART has developed a comprehensive IP commercialisation ecosystem that spans multiple industries, deepening emotional bonds between fans and IPs while exploring broader opportunities for value creation. Our ventures include the world’s first immersive intellectual property themed park in the art toy industry, the expansion of its IPs into global markets through licensing and co-branding with leading global brands, and the curation of art toy expos and intellectual property themed exhibitions internationally. In Europe, POP MART has built a strong presence in key markets including the United Kingdom, France, Italy, Germany, the Netherlands and Spain, and is expanding into new high potential markets. Our expansion brings POP MART closer to European fans and offers new ways for them to connect with our IPs and stories.




For more information, please visit www.popmart.com and our LinkedIn Career page.




What to Expect




POP MART is a fast-paced company with a young and multicultural team where you can expect to:




Contribute to a fast-growing European business that combines retail, art, and creative storytelling
Work with passionate teams across different countries, all connected by a shared love for designer toys and unique customer experiences
Grow your skills through hands-on projects, collaborative learning, and opportunities to explore new ideas




Benefits




POP MART offers our employees:




Monthly appreciation gifts featuring our latest POP MART designs
A structured bonus scheme linked to both company and individual performance
25 days of annual leave, in addition to bank holidays
4x life insurance, together with group medical and travel insurance
A confidential Employee Assistance Programme, alongside access to a discount platform for selected online retailers
Training and development programmes, together with regular team events that encourage learning, collaboration, and celebration




The Role and Candidate




We are looking for a data driven and detail oriented professional to support business performance monitoring and analysis across POP MART’s European business.

This role focuses on operational reporting, performance tracking, and business insight generation across key revenue channels, including physical stores, vending machines, and online business. You will support the business by monitoring core operational indicators, identifying trends and issues through data, and helping teams better understand performance through clear, accurate, and practical analysis.

You will also work closely with teams including Finance, Retail Operations, Marketing, Merchandising, and Business Development to support regular reporting, operational reviews, and day to day data requests. In parallel, the role will contribute to improving reporting consistency, data quality, and the practical use of data in business operations.

This position would suit someone with solid analytical capability, structured thinking, and a hands on approach, who is comfortable working with detailed data while supporting wider business needs in a fast paced environment.




Responsibilities




Daily Operation Monitoring and Performance Review




Establish daily, weekly and monthly monitoring for core operational indicators, including revenue, gross profit, expense ratio, payment collection, labour efficiency and store efficiency, across all revenue channels such as physical stores, vending machines and online business
Prepare daily, weekly, monthly and quarterly operational reports in a timely manner, benchmark performance against budget, year on year and month on month data, and analyse the causes of performance gaps
Track key business indicators including sales volume, average transaction value, conversion rate and profit margin, and provide regular operational analysis to support performance improvement
Analyse the performance of different markets, stores and vending machines, identify trends and issues, and support the development of targeted actions to improve profitability
Support the Business Development team with data input for store and vending machine site selection, based on market data and operational analysis
Support monthly and quarterly operational review meetings by preparing review materials and presentations, highlighting business progress, key issues and improvement priorities, and tracking follow up actions
Work closely with the Finance team to verify operational, cost and expense data, align statistical definitions, resolve data discrepancies, and ensure management reporting is accurate and reliable




Business Insights and Reporting




Work closely with business teams on a daily basis to understand key business pain points, and support practical data solutions based on business needs and operating scenarios
Conduct data analysis across sales operations, marketing and consumer insights, identify the business logic behind the data, and provide insights to support business optimisation
Improve the quality and depth of recurring insight reports by moving beyond data listing and strengthening business focused conclusions and recommendations
Build and maintain structured data records and archives to support data traceability, consistency and reusability
Extract and process data from multiple sources, including business systems, CRM and ERP systems, and use SQL for regular queries, data cleaning, deduplication, correlation and aggregation
Build and maintain basic dashboards and automated reports to reduce manual reporting, improve data visibility, and support more efficient access to information




Cross Functional Collaboration and Data Governance




Support Sales, Marketing, Product, Supply Chain, Finance and other teams with day to day data requests, including customised queries, report exports and basic analysis support
Provide guidance to business teams on basic data viewing and analysis methods to improve the practical use of data in daily operations
Support the standardisation of data fields, statistical rules and analysis templates, and help establish data logs and archiving practices to strengthen data governance
Contribute to the development of a more consistent and practical data culture across the business




Required Qualifications




3–5 years of business analysis experience; candidates with experience in management consulting, internet business analysis, retail operations analysis, or other relevant analytical experience, or those with extensive professional experience are preferred




Excellent communication and organisational skills, capable of handling complex issues with structured thinking and analytical abilities




Proactive, hands-on, and entrepreneurial mindset with experience working in a fast-paced business environment




Outstanding problem-solving, analytical, written and verbal communication skills




Data-sensitive, able to identify business trends and issues through data analysis; proficiency in data analysis and visualisation tools such as Excel, SQL, Tableau is preferred, but strong willingness to learn is a must




Proficiency in English (listening, speaking, reading and writing), with extensive experience in cross-team project collaboration and communication







Desirable but Not Essential




Experience in the toy industry or pop culture retail sector
Familiarity with Local and European retail landscapes
Understanding of designer toy and collectible trends, particularly in the art toy market
Experience supporting operational reporting across multiple countries or regions
Understanding of retail KPIs including conversion rate, ATV, margin, labour efficiency and inventory performance
Exposure to data governance, reporting automation, or process improvement initiatives
Experience within retail, consumer goods, eCommerce, or multi-site operations environments
Experience working with ERP, CRM, POS or business intelligence systems
Familiarity with dashboard tools such as Tableau, Power BI or Looker










If you are excited to be part of POP MART’s journey and believe you have the skills and passion to contribute, click Apply Now and send us your CV.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Meet the Team

Cisco ThousandEyes is a Digital Assurance platform that empowers organizations to deliver flawless digital experiences across every network – even the ones they don’t own. Powered by AI and an unmatched set of cloud, internet and enterprise network telemetry data, ThousandEyes enables IT teams to proactively detect, diagnose, and remediate issues – before they impact end- user experiences.

ThousandEyes is deeply integrated across the entire Cisco technology portfolio and beyond, helping customers deploy at scale while also delivering AI-powered assurance insights within Cisco’s leading Networking, Security, Collaboration, and Observability portfolios.

Your Impact

You will employ analytical techniques to solve key questions that make the business of Cisco ThousandEyes successful. You will be primarily responsibly to produce statistical reports that covers analyses reporting internet performance for Government customers.


Lead key, cross-functional analytical projects, identifying and bringing together stakeholders/ partners, developing joint analytical approaches, and completing the project.
Collaborate with internal and external partners, including Data Scientists within the company and across clients, on methodology for reporting that performance of ISPs in specific geographies and to explain trends and patterns in the data.
Develop and maintains data pipelines and dashboards to monitor and track trends and changes in the customer data.
Use data to find opportunities and patterns that Government customers may be interested in.
Display deep data wrangling techniques, strong visualization skills to depict/ present narratives that identify growth opportunities.


Minimum Qualifications


Strong R for production reporting pipelines: tidyverse, multi-file project navigation, debugging across pipeline stages, and package/version management with renv.
SQL for analytical querying against BigQuery and MariaDB via bigrquery, DBI, and RMariaDB.
Literate programming and reporting: R Markdown and Quarto rendering to Word, chart/table formatting with ggplot2/flextable/officedown, and reproducible quarterly output.
Statistical judgment: interpreting confidence intervals, weighted means, trimmed means, and sample size limitations - knowing when a result is technically accurate but misleading.
Report writing for a regulatory audience: clear, detailed, defensible prose with consistent terminology, informative tone, and editorial judgment that AI drafting alone can't replace.
Attention to detail in published outputs: catching mislabelled figures, wrong baselines, numbers that don't sum, and chart axes that obscure trends.
Data management across multiple systems: BigQuery, GCS, SharePoint/OneDrive, and local R sessions with no automated orchestration -- manual, script-by-script execution requiring care and rigor.
Comfortable using AI coding tools (Cursor, Copilot, etc.) as a force-multiplier for pipeline maintenance, debugging, drafting, and routine code changes.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with broadband measurement, telecom/ISP data, or network performance concepts.
Spatial data basics in R.
Awareness of PII and data confidentiality constraints.
Shiny for small internal tools.


Why Cisco?

At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint.

Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere.

We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

Title: Digital Marketing Data Analyst - Fashion Retail
Description:
The Role

A client in the global retail and fashion industry is seeking a Digital Marketing Data Analyst to join the team for an initial contract engagement.

You will work closely with senior stakeholders across marketing and analytics to support data‑driven decision making. The objective of the role is to analyse digital marketing performance, generate actionable insights, and help optimise media and campaign effectiveness.




Responsibilities

Analyse digital marketing performance across paid media, CRM, and web analytics channels
Build and maintain dashboards and reports to track KPIs and campaign effectiveness

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Airtime is a leading data-driven rewards platform, transforming how consumers save and how retailers connect with their customers. Our app is used by millions of members, helping them reduce everyday costs while delivering measurable value back to our retail partners.




We work with a wide range of well-known brands, using our proprietary technology, real-time transaction data and advanced analytics to power smarter, more effective performance marketing. By combining data, insight and creativity, we enable retailers to better understand and engage their customers, driving incremental revenue and long-term loyalty.




Our platform delivers a seamless, best-in-class experience for both members and partners, and we continue to invest in new features and capabilities to support our growth and ambition.




At Airtime, we are driven by innovation and this is what keeps us ahead. We are a team with a shared ambition to succeed, holding ourselves to high standards while working collaboratively to achieve our goals




The Opportunity




Airtime is looking for a Data Analyst with 3+ years of experience in a data analytics role. This is an opportunity for someone who can move beyond basic reporting and play a meaningful role in driving partner performance and commercial decisions across the business.




You’ll work across multiple areas including Sales, Marketing, Product, and Finance, giving you broad exposure to how the business operates. Alongside supporting ongoing analysis across a range of business needs, you’ll focus on shaping problems, uncovering insights, and delivering clear, actionable recommendations that support strategy and performance.




Reporting to the Analytics Manager, you will:




Analyse performance data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities across multiple areas of the business
Partner with stakeholders across Sales, Marketing, Product, and Finance to shape problems and deliver actionable insights that drive decision-making
Translate business questions into structured analysis and clear, actionable recommendations
Communicate complex data through clear, compelling narratives tailored to both technical and non-technical audiences
Develop a strong understanding of Airtime’s product, platform, and commercial model to provide context-rich analysis
Proactively identify opportunities to optimise performance, improve processes, and support business growth
Support ongoing analysis across a range of business needs, ensuring insights are consistent and relevant
Contribute to improving data quality, definitions, and consistency across the business




Requirements:

3+ years of experience in a data analytics role
Experience working cross-functionally and building relationships with stakeholders across different business areas
Strong commercial awareness and understanding of how data impacts business performance
Experience translating business requests or problems into structured analysis and actionable recommendations
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience working with large and complex datasets
Proficiency in SQL (BigQuery a plus), Excel, and data visualisation tools
Strong communication and storytelling skills, with experience presenting insights and influencing decision-making
Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Curious, proactive, and motivated to use data to drive improvements
Able to commute to the Manchester office twice per week




What You Will Get

We believe great work happens when people feel supported, trusted, and genuinely valued. So we’ve built a benefits package that’s designed to give you flexibility, support your wellbeing, and help you grow, both inside and outside of work.




Reward & Security

Share options, be part of our success
Competitive salary
Life assurance at 5x your salary




Time Off That Matters

23 days holiday, plus more with every year you’re here (up to 28)
Your birthday off, have a ‘you day’
Buy extra holiday when you need it (up to 5 extra days)
A dedicated charity day to give back




Flexibility & Balance

Flexible start times (anytime between 6:30–10:30am)
Hybrid working to balance home and office life
A modern, central office space when you want to collaborate in person




Health & Wellbeing

Private medical insurance
Health cash plan for everyday healthcare
Virtual GP access for you and your family
24/7 support for mental health, counselling, and wellbeing




Growth & Development

Learning & development budget and dedicated time to use it
Professional accreditation funding
Real opportunities to learn from experienced colleagues and grow your career




Support Through Life

Enhanced maternity, paternity & adoption leave
A culture that understands life happens outside of work




Community & Culture

Company charity contributions
Regular team moments that actually feel meaningful (not forced fun)
A team that values collaboration, curiosity, and getting things done




The Bottom Line

We’re building a place where you can do your best work without burning out, standing still, or feeling like just another cog in the machine.




Because when you’re supported, everything else follows.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: City Of London, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Who are we?👋

Look at the latest headlines and you will see something Ki insures. Think space shuttles, world tours, wind farms, and even footballers' legs. 


Ki's mission is simple. Digitally disrupt and revolutionise a 335-year-old market. Working with Google and UCL, Ki has created a platform that uses algorithms, machine learning and large language models to give insurance brokers quotes in seconds, rather than days. 


Ki is proudly the biggest global algorithmic insurance carrier. It is the fastest growing syndicate in the Lloyd's of London market, and the first ever to make $100m in profit in 3 years. 


Ki's teams have varied backgrounds and work together in an agile, cross-functional way to build the very best experience for its customers. Ki has big ambitions but needs more excellent minds to challenge the status-quo and help it reach new horizons.


Where you come in?

The Data Analyst at Ki works at the intersection of analytics, engineering and product. They work within teams and across Ki's teams to support and drive design, development, and optimization of analytics, data models, reports, and other data-driven products that support strategic business goals.  


What you will be doing: 🖋️


Applied Analytics

Develop knowledge and expertise in Ki's business domains to generate insights and measurement on initiatives that drive company objectives.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to identify opportunities for leveraging data analytics to drive commercial results and improve operational efficiency.
Identify opportunities to leverage data analytics techniques to extract value from both internal and external data assets to enrich analytical capabilities.


Analytics Engineering

Develop and maintain the core data sets underlying the suite of analytical tools used by the team
Create and maintain data visualizations and dashboards using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Develop scalable data solutions that conform to Ki's technology and engineering principles and industry best practice.


Data Management & Governance

Preserve the integrity of data, making sure it is accurate, consistent, and reliable throughout its lifecycle.
Investigate and lead timely resolution of emerging issues with underlying data systems and models as and when these arise.
Ensure that domain data is managed and used according to Ki's data governance policy and best practice principles.


Other

Ensure all data systems and models are documented in line with Ki wide standards
Recommend ways to improve data efficiency and reliability
Increase the degree of automation within the team's data systems and tools
Investigate new tools that would help the team store, structure and analyse data.


Requirements


Proficiency in SQL and Python to manipulate and analyse datasets 
Experience with data reporting and visualisation tools (e.g. Tableau, Looker, Dash, Streamlit) 
Solid understanding of statistical techniques (e.g. regression, clustering, correlation analysis), with ability to apply these to commercial problem solving. 
Experience working in cloud native environments (especially GCP) would be plus. 
Experience with designing and implementation of data structures to ensure ease of use and accessibility by a broader audience. 
Self-organised, good at documenting and communicating findings and approach. 
Excellent communicator, able to present complex analysis to both technical and non-technical audiences. 
Experience working in a regulated industry


What to expect during the recruitment process:

Initial recruiter screening call
Interview with hiring manager
Technical Interview (this may vary depending on the role)
Values Interview

Title: Data Analyst
Place: City Of London, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Analyst, Investment Data Services UK

Location: London, King's Cross (Hybrid)

Employment Type: Full‑Time, Permanent

Enable high‑quality investment decision‑making through trusted, well‑governed data.

Work at the intersection of investments, data platforms, and governance in a global asset owner.

Flexible hybrid working to support your wellbeing and work-life balance.



At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members. We are dedicated to building a high-performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.



About Us

AustralianSuper's purpose is simple: to help members achieve their best possible retirement outcomes. As one of the world's largest pension funds, operating within Australia's $3.9 trillion superannuation industry, we are navigating a rapidly evolving landscape shaped by regulation, technology, and changing member expectations.

Our Data Services function is a newly established capability responsible for building a best‑in‑class data governance, management, and operations framework across the Fund. Based in London, this role supports the global investment function by ensuring the accurate, timely, and reliable delivery of investment data to decision‑makers and analytical teams.



The Opportunity

This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing investment data capability at a pivotal stage of its development. As an Analyst, Investment Data Services UK, you'll play a key role in enabling trusted investment data across front‑office, operational, and analytical teams.

You'll work across data acquisition, mastering, production, and governance - operating as a subject matter expert on investment data platforms and collaborating closely with technology, investment, and operations stakeholders.

You'll have the opportunity to:


Undertake daily control tasks and monitor database updates to ensure accurate and timely data delivery.

Troubleshoot data pipeline disruptions with technology teams and communicate impacts to downstream users.

Validate security setups in fund data warehouse systems, translating term sheets and loan agreements, and updating systems with correct values.

Maintain portfolio setups and internal structures (fund & pricing hierarchies) across platforms.

Identify, investigate, and resolve data exceptions and quality issues; escalate as needed and represent the team in issue management forums.

Perform investment data interrogation and reconciliations using modern tools; investigate breaks with operational teams and vendors.

Liaise with data vendors, custodians, and investment teams to resolve data and reporting issues, ensuring SLAs are met.

Evaluate business requests for new data and participate in design, implementation, and testing of data solutions.

Recommend and implement improvements to data collection, maintenance, timeliness, and reporting.

Act as a subject matter expert in sourcing, validating, and governing data.

Identify and apply new technologies to improve data management and reporting processes (including Microsoft Power Automate).

Operate on ad hoc data management and reporting requests from the Data Services department.

Nurture and maintain internal relationships across all levels of the Fund, collaborating locally and internationally.

Ensure compliance with relevant legislative and organisational requirements, and advocate for continuous improvement in governance and risk management.



What You'll Bring

You're a detail‑oriented and analytically minded data professional who enjoys working with complex investment data, improving data quality, and partnering across teams to deliver meaningful outcomes.

To succeed in this role, you'll bring:

Experience in investment management or asset owner within a data operations capability.

Strong understanding of financial markets and instruments across multiple asset classes.

Experience with investment and data platforms such as Finbourne LUSID and Informatica.

Exposure to market and investment platforms such as Aladdin (highly desirable), MSCI BarraOne, S&P Ilevel, or Calypso (essential).

Solid understanding of ETL processes, data acquisition, and data mastering concepts.

Comprehensive knowledge of the differences between IBOR and ABOR and their application within asset management environments.

Working knowledge of SQL, Python, and APIs.

Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to influence through data knowledge and attention to detail.

A collaborative mindset and the ability to contribute effectively within cross‑functional teams.



Qualifications:

Tertiary qualifications in STEM, Commerce, Finance, Accounting, or a numerate discipline (mandatory).

CFA (or candidate) and data science training are viewed favourably.



Why Join Us?

At AustralianSuper, we're committed to creating an inclusive, flexible, and high‑performing environment where everyone can contribute meaningfully.

You'll benefit from:

A flexible hybrid working model designed to support balance and wellbeing.

The opportunity to work at the heart of a global investment organisation.

Exposure to complex, enterprise‑scale investment data platforms and governance frameworks.

Ongoing professional development and career growth opportunities.

The chance to make a real impact by enabling high‑quality investment decisions that support millions of members.



What's Next?

If you're passionate about investment data, governance, and enabling better decision‑making at scale, we'd love to hear from you.

At AustralianSuper, we strive for an inclusive recruitment process. If you require adjustments at any stage, please let us know.

Shape the future. Lead with purpose. Grow with AustralianSuper.

Progress, powered by purpose.



AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia, the UK and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy.



https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice.

Title: Data & Analytics Analyst
Place: Kingston Upon Thames, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Manpower are currently seeking an interim Data & Analytics Analyst to work with our global FMCG client The Magnum Ice Cream Company, renowned for brands such as Magnum, Ben & Jerry's, Cornetto and Wall's, and become an integral part of their fast-paced FMCG environment.




The position is based in Kingston upon Thames, Surrey. This is a full-time temporary role to run for 6 months, requiring 36.25 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Compensation for this role is competitive.




The role currently offers a mix of remote and onsite working, subject to adjustment based on business requirements.







Data & Analytics Analyst – Wholesaler Sales

The Magnum Ice Cream Company is on a mission to create the ultimate snacking company. A place where growth, innovation, founder and ownership mentality, and performance are cultivated and rewarded. And where we craft extraordinary ice cream experiences - transforming ordinary moments into lasting memories. Because we know, life tastes better with ice cream.




ABOUT THE MAGNUM ICE CREAM COMPANY

The Magnum Ice Cream Company is all about growth. Growing our business. Growing our customers’ businesses. Growing our people’s careers. Growth begins with empowerment. So, we free our people to be innovative, responsible entrepreneurs, driven and equipped to give our consumers more amazing products and unforgettable moments – and having fun doing it. With 19,000 expert ice cream colleagues and iconic brands like Wall’s, Cornetto and Ben & Jerry’s, loved in 76 countries, we are the world’s largest Ice Cream company leading the industry.




Position Overview

We’re looking for a Data & Analytics Analyst to unlock the value of wholesaler and customer sales data across Magnum’s UK business.




You will play a critical role in connecting multiple wholesaler data sources, working with Databricks and pipelines, and transforming raw sales data into clear, actionable insights. This includes enabling visibility on sell-in, sell-out, distribution, range, and performance across wholesale partners.




You will work closely with data engineers to ensure data is ingested, structured, and ready for analysis, while acting as a Power BI super user to deliver dashboards that drive commercial decisions across sales, account management, and operations.




Key Responsibilities

Connect & Integrate Wholesaler Data

Connect and consolidate multiple wholesaler sales data sources (including but not limited to EPOS, shipment/sell-in, CRM, partner data)
Work with Databricks to query, combine, and transform large-scale sales datasets
Connect to and leverage data pipelines to access reliable, up-to-date wholesaler data
Partner with data engineers to onboard new wholesale partners and data feeds
Ensure consistent, accurate mapping of SKU, customer, and channel data




Data Ingestion & Pipeline Collaboration

Support and oversee data ingestion processes for wholesaler and partner data
Work closely with data engineers to bring in new data sources and improve data availability
Validate and sense-check incoming data to ensure accuracy and completeness
Help ensure data pipelines deliver timely, structured, analysis-ready datasets

Data Visualisation & Commercial Insights

Act as a Power BI super user, building dashboards focused on wholesale performance
Deliver reporting on key commercial metrics (e.g. sales trends, distribution, range compliance, customer performance)
Create intuitive data visualisations for sales and account teams
Translate complex sales data into clear insights and actions

Make Data Usable for the Business

Develop clean, structured datasets to support sales, CRM, and commercial reporting
Contribute to a single source of truth for wholesaler and customer data
Ensure consistency in definitions (e.g. sales metrics, customer views, SKUs)
Enable self-serve analytics for commercial teams

Set Us Up for Success

Support scalable solutions that accommodate new wholesalers and growing data complexity
Contribute to data documentation, definitions, and data dictionaries
Ensure high standards of data quality, governance, and trust
Identify opportunities to improve data coverage, accuracy, and reporting

Business Partnering & Opportunity Identification

Work closely with sales, account managers, and CRM teams to answer key business questions
Identify trends and opportunities within wholesaler performance and distribution
Proactively highlight risks and opportunities (e.g. gaps in distribution, declining accounts, growth pockets)
Translate business needs into analytical solutions




Required Skills

Advanced Power BI expertise (data modelling, DAX, dashboard delivery)
Experience working with Databricks desirable (data querying, transformation)
Strong SQL skills desirable and ability to work with large, complex sales datasets

Manpower are currently seeking an interim Data & Analytics Analyst to work with our global FMCG client The Magnum Ice Cream Company, renowned for brands such as Magnum, Ben & Jerry's, Cornetto and Wall's, and become an integral part of their fast-paced FMCG environment.


The position is based in Kingston upon Thames, Surrey. This is a full-time temporary role to run for 6 months, requiring 36.25 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Compensation for this role is competitive.


The role currently offers a mix of remote and onsite working, subject to adjustment based on business requirements.



Data & Analytics Analyst – Wholesaler Sales

The Magnum Ice Cream Company is on a mission to create the ultimate snacking company. A place where growth, innovation, founder and ownership mentality, and performance are cultivated and rewarded. And where we craft extraordinary ice cream experiences - transforming ordinary moments into lasting memories. Because we know, life tastes better with ice cream.


ABOUT THE MAGNUM ICE CREAM COMPANY

The Magnum Ice Cream Company is all about growth. Growing our business. Growing our customers’ businesses. Growing our people’s careers. Growth begins with empowerment. So, we free our people to be innovative, responsible entrepreneurs, driven and equipped to give our consumers more amazing products and unforgettable moments – and having fun doing it. With 19,000 expert ice cream colleagues and iconic brands like Wall’s, Cornetto and Ben & Jerry’s, loved in 76 countries, we are the world’s largest Ice Cream company leading the industry.


Position Overview

We’re looking for a Data & Analytics Analyst to unlock the value of wholesaler and customer sales data across Magnum’s UK business.


You will play a critical role in connecting multiple wholesaler data sources, working with Databricks and pipelines, and transforming raw sales data into clear, actionable insights. This includes enabling visibility on sell-in, sell-out, distribution, range, and performance across wholesale partners.


You will work closely with data engineers to ensure data is ingested, structured, and ready for analysis, while acting as a Power BI super user to deliver dashboards that drive commercial decisions across sales, account management, and operations.


Key Responsibilities

Connect & Integrate Wholesaler Data

  • Connect and consolidate multiple wholesaler sales data sources (including but not limited to EPOS, shipment/sell-in, CRM, partner data)
  • Work with Databricks to query, combine, and transform large-scale sales datasets
  • Connect to and leverage data pipelines to access reliable, up-to-date wholesaler data
  • Partner with data engineers to onboard new wholesale partners and data feeds
  • Ensure consistent, accurate mapping of SKU, customer, and channel data


Data Ingestion & Pipeline Collaboration

  • Support and oversee data ingestion processes for wholesaler and partner data
  • Work closely with data engineers to bring in new data sources and improve data availability
  • Validate and sense-check incoming data to ensure accuracy and completeness
  • Help ensure data pipelines deliver timely, structured, analysis-ready datasets

Data Visualisation & Commercial Insights

  • Act as a Power BI super user, building dashboards focused on wholesale performance
  • Deliver reporting on key commercial metrics (e.g. sales trends, distribution, range compliance, customer performance)
  • Create intuitive data visualisations for sales and account teams
  • Translate complex sales data into clear insights and actions

Make Data Usable for the Business

  • Develop clean, structured datasets to support sales, CRM, and commercial reporting
  • Contribute to a single source of truth for wholesaler and customer data
  • Ensure consistency in definitions (e.g. sales metrics, customer views, SKUs)
  • Enable self-serve analytics for commercial teams

Set Us Up for Success

  • Support scalable solutions that accommodate new wholesalers and growing data complexity
  • Contribute to data documentation, definitions, and data dictionaries
  • Ensure high standards of data quality, governance, and trust
  • Identify opportunities to improve data coverage, accuracy, and reporting

Business Partnering & Opportunity Identification

  • Work closely with sales, account managers, and CRM teams to answer key business questions
  • Identify trends and opportunities within wholesaler performance and distribution
  • Proactively highlight risks and opportunities (e.g. gaps in distribution, declining accounts, growth pockets)
  • Translate business needs into analytical solutions


Required Skills

  • Advanced Power BI expertise (data modelling, DAX, dashboard delivery)
  • Experience working with Databricks desirable (data querying, transformation)
  • Strong SQL skills desirable and ability to work with large, complex sales datasets
  • Experience with data pipelines and ingestion processes
  • Proven ability to connect and combine multiple data sources
  • Experience working with sales, wholesaler, or FMCG data is highly desirable
  • Strong understanding of commercial metrics and reporting
  • Experience collaborating with data engineers and non-technical stakeholders
  • Commercially minded with strong curiosity for sales performance drivers
  • Detail-oriented with a strong focus on data accuracy and consistency
  • Proactive and solutions-driven
  • Ability to manage conflicting priorities and short deadlines
  • Strong communicator, able to translate data into actionable business insight


Success in This Role Looks Like

  • Seamless integration of wholesaler sales data across multiple partners
  • Trusted, high-quality datasets supporting commercial decisions
  • Widely adopted Power BI dashboards across sales and account teams
  • Strong collaboration between analytics, engineering, and commercial teams
  • Clear visibility of wholesaler performance, risks, and growth opportunities

Title: Graduate Data Analyst
Description:
Sika is a specialty chemicals company with a globally leading position in the development and production of systems and products for bonding, sealing, damping, reinforcing and protection in the building sector and industry. Sika has subsidiaries in 103 countries, produces in over 400 factories, and approximately 33,000 employees generated CHF 11.20 billion in sales in 2025.



At Sika, we combine expertise, innovation and teamwork to help build the modern world. Our products support projects and industries ranging from homes and infrastructure to renewable energy, transport and advanced manufacturing.





Job Description



This opportunity sits within our Operations function and supports a growing portfolio of data-driven improvement projects across Customer Services and Sales & Demand Planning. The role has been created to strengthen internal analytical capability and help teams turn data into practical improvements across day-to-day operations.



As our Graduate Data Analyst, you’ll help improve visibility, decision-making and performance across key operational processes. This role is ideal for someone who already has a foundation in data analytics and is looking for the chance to apply those skills in a live business environment where insight can quickly become action. Based on the business rationale, the role is focused on improving analytical capability and supporting a pipeline of data-led operational improvement initiatives.



What’s in it for you?



The Opportunity:



You’ll join a role designed to build analytical capability across the business while giving you exposure to genuine operational challenges and improvement initiatives. From day one, you’ll work on live projects that matter and contribute to how we manage service, reporting and planning performance.



You’ll support and contribute to a range of important initiatives including:



Order Book Management
Power BI Dashboard development and reporting
Delivery Performance Live Reporting
Performance Metrics and Standardisation
Identification of Automation and AI opportunities across processes

You’ll also have the opportunity to complete a newly formed Level 4 AI and Automation Practitioner Apprenticeship, giving you a strong platform for both technical development and future progression.



The Rewards:



You will be rewarded with a competitive salary and a sweep of great benefits. We pride ourselves on our total reward package:



Holiday 25 days rising to 28 days with service, plus bank holidays
A company pension contribution of up to 10%
Private healthcare for you, with the option to add family
Group life cover at 6x your annual salary
Global Income Protection (PHI)
Enhanced maternity and paternity pay
Staff discount platform & Cycle to Work scheme
Dedicated Service Awards for reaching key milestones
Selected discounted Sika products

The Support:



This role is designed as a development opportunity, giving you exposure to a broad operational environment while working on real business priorities. Supported by a mentor, you will identify key learning goals which align to your specific career goals and skills requirement. You’ll be part of a business that values learning, collaboration and continuous improvement, with structured support and development from the outset.





Qualifications



The Skills:



You’ll gain hands-on experience in data analysis, reporting, insight generation and business improvement in a role that connects analytics to real operational outcomes. You’ll also develop your understanding of how data supports service, planning and performance improvement across the wider business.





Additional Information



The Future:



This role offers an excellent foundation for a longer-term career in analytics, operations and continuous improvement within a global organisation.



What we need from you?



We’re looking for someone with a background in data analytics, or a closely related discipline, who is eager to turn data into insight and insight into action. You’ll be naturally curious, enjoy solving problems and want to make a genuine contribution early in your career.



You’ll likely bring:



A solid grounding in data analytics and an interest in using data to improve business performance
Curiosity around reporting, systems, data visualisation and automation
The confidence to work across teams and translate analysis into practical insight
A proactive attitude and the drive to take ownership of your development
Good communication skills and a collaborative way of working
A genuine interest in building a career in analytics, operations or continuous improvement

We offer competitive salaries, aligned with local market benchmarks and the specific scope and responsibilities of each role. Compensation is determined based skills relevant to the position, education and/or training. We are committed to fair and equitable pay practices in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
A specialist investment management firm is looking for a Data Analyst to join its London-based team. Managing approximately $4bn in assets, the firm offers a unique opportunity to gain exposure across investments, client relations, and operations within a highly collaborative environment.




Working directly with the Head of Data Analytics, you will support the business through data analysis, reporting, data quality management, and the development of internal tools and processes. You will also contribute to client reporting, presentations, RFPs, DDQs, and operational activities including trade reporting and reconciliations.




This is a hands-on role suited to someone who enjoys solving problems, working with data, and taking ownership of a broad range of responsibilities. The successful candidate will gain exposure to all areas of the business, offering a level of responsibility and learning rarely available in larger organisations.







Requirements:

Strong degree in a quantitative discipline such as Mathematics, Engineering, Physics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related subject
Strong Excel skills
Interest and experience in SQL, Python, automation, and data analytics
Excellent attention to detail and analytical thinking
Ability to manage a varied workload and work across multiple business functions
Interest in financial markets and investing




Due to the increased number of applications for this role, we may only respond to candidates whose experience most closely matches the criteria.

At our company, we prioritise equity throughout the recruitment process. We are committed to ensuring fairness and equal opportunities for all applicants. If there is anything we can do to make the process more accessible to you, please let us know.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
hackajob is collaborating with AXA to connect them with exceptional professionals for this role.

About AXA

AXA is a global leader in insurance and financial services, dedicated to helping customers protect what matters most to them. As the sixth-largest insurance company in the world, we provide a wide range of services, including health, car, home, and business insurance. We support millions of customers worldwide, helping them navigate life's uncertainties with confidence.

AXA Commercial protects businesses, from multinationals to micro start-ups, giving them the confidence to thrive.

Job Overview

Are you passionate about turning big data into actionable insights? Join our Commercial Pricing Data Team at AXA, where you’ll play a key role in shaping our strategic data landscape. Working closely with colleagues across different teams, you’ll help build a robust, insightful, and accessible data environment that empowers our Pricing Analysts to make informed decisions. If you’re eager to make a real impact through data and enjoy collaborative, innovative work, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

Key Responsibilities


Work with a variety of data tools and databases to design solutions, as well as maintaining, creating, updating, reading, and deleting data as needed.
Collect data from multiple sources, handling raw data, cleansing it, and converting it into structured formats suitable for further analysis and data science activities.
Develop and maintain a business glossary and data dictionaries, and classifying data in accordance with local priorities.
Collaborate with Data Architects, Data Protection Officers, and other stakeholders to implement data-by-design processes.
Build expertise and capacity within the pricing team to enable self-service access to data from the data lake in the most efficient way.
Create dynamic reports within Unity Catalog and Power BI.
Eliminate outdated reports and processes and respond swiftly to provide the right data with ease, thereby supporting better pricing decisions and helping us stay ahead as a market leader.


At AXA we work smart, empowering our people to balance their time between home and the office in a way that works best for them, their team and our customers. You'll work at least two days a week (40%) away from home, moving to three days a week (60%) in the future. Away from home means attending the office, visiting clients or attending industry events.

Your Skills & Experience


Proficient in Python with experience using PySpark, DevOps, and Unity Catalog.
Demonstrated skills in extracting, manipulating, and reconciling big data sets.
Ability to work across structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data, extracting insights and identifying connections between disparate data sets.
Solid understanding of information security principles to ensure data is handled and managed in a compliant manner.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to clearly explain complex solutions to diverse audiences.
Understanding of the Commercial Insurance sector would be advantageous.
Familiarity with Acturis would be advantageous.

Title: Graduate Data Analyst
Description:
Stubben Edge’s mission is to make financial services simple, affordable, and accessible. We’re looking for a Data Analyst to join our team in a hands-on role, responsible for deriving actionable insights from data to support the design, analysis, and management of our innovative insurance products.




What you’ll do:

Analyse large and complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights into customer behaviour and product performance.
Design, build, and maintain reports and dashboards to monitor key performance indicators for our products and markets.
Collaborate closely with our Product, Engineering, and Sales teams to support their data needs and help them make data-driven decisions.
Ensure the accuracy and integrity of the data within our core platforms, including FlightDeck.
Translate data-driven insights into clear and compelling reports and presentations for stakeholders.
Support the entire product lifecycle with robust data analysis, from initial research and development to launch and ongoing performance management.




What we’re looking for:

A recent graduate with a 2:2 degree or above in a quantitative field such as Data Science, Engineering, Computer Science, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related discipline.
A strong academic background with demonstrated analytical and problem-solving skills.
An excellent communicator, comfortable with explaining complex data to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Some relevant work experience (such as an internship, placement, or graduate-level role) is highly desirable.
A keen interest in applying data to solve business problems, particularly within the insurance and financial services industry.
Strong organisational skills and high attention to detail.
Proactive, intellectually curious, and eager to learn in a fast-paced environment.
Must have the right to work in the UK and be able to work from our London office 3 days a week.




What we offer:

25 days’ holiday plus bank holidays.
A 37.5-hour working week.
Comprehensive training, development opportunities, and the chance to grow your career in a fast-moving fintech.




If you’re ambitious, data-driven, and ready to build your career in financial services, we’d love to hear from you.
